sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: 6 Reasons Why Digital Brands Group Investors Are Smiling Today as DBGI Stock Soars
feed.summary: Digital Brands Group (DBGI) stock soared on Tuesday following an updated guidance for fiscal year 2022, projecting revenue between $37.5 million and $42.5 million. This forecast represents a 350% increase from 2021 guidance and anticipates positive EBITDA, driven by expanded marketing, Amazon sales, new products, showrooms, and wholesale revenue. The company also plans for growth through acquisitions.

feed.title: Turismo City se amplía y compró empresa brasilera Quanto Custa Viajar con una inversión de u$s6 millones
feed.summary: Turismocity has expanded its operations by acquiring the Brazilian travel company Quanto Custa Viajar for $6 million. This investment round included participation from prominent executives like Eduardo Elsztain and Martin Migoya, aiming to solidify Turismocity's position as a leading global travel company. The acquisition follows Turismocity's earlier purchase of Farecompare and seeks to leverage synergies to grow its presence in the Brazilian market.

feed.title: 1stDibs just released an e-commerce report. Here’s where they think online luxury is headed
feed.summary: 1stDibs has released an e-commerce report highlighting the significant growth in the online luxury shopping market. The report indicates a substantial increase in 1stDibs's gross market value and active buyers, demonstrating the comfort consumers now have with purchasing high-value items online. While furniture remains its largest overall category, the company's highest individual sales are in art and jewelry, with a noteworthy average order value exceeding $2,500.

feed.title: Lauren Hobart: age, children, husband, education, career, salary, profiles, net worth
feed.summary: Lauren Hobart is the CEO of Dick's Sporting Goods, taking over in 2021 and driving significant growth and strategic planning for the company. With over 25 years in finance, retail, and consumer experience, she previously held various roles at Dick's, including Senior Vice President and CMO. Hobart has also been instrumental in initiatives like the Sports Matter program and successfully navigated the company through the 2020 economic and health crises, earning a reported compensation of over $6.7 million in 2019 and an estimated net worth of $46.5 million as of 2021.

feed.title: Etsy debuts shoppable virtual house
feed.summary: Etsy has launched "Etsy House," an interactive augmented reality experience allowing shoppers to tour a digital home decorated with marketplace items. This initiative builds on Etsy's previous AR offerings and positions it alongside other retailers like David's Bridal, Asos, and Amazon, who are utilizing AR and VR to enhance product visualization and e-commerce. The company has also recently expanded its business through acquisitions of Depop and Elo7.

feed.title: Can LEO Meet the Changing Arctic’s Connectivity Needs?
feed.summary: The Arctic's rapidly changing environmental and geopolitical landscape is increasing its strategic importance, driving a demand for enhanced connectivity. Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites are emerging as a crucial solution to provide reliable communication in this data-starved region, where traditional Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites struggle. The U.S. government, through initiatives like the AFRL's DEUCSI program, is collaborating with commercial LEO operators such as Iridium, OneWeb, and SpaceX to deploy and test robust satellite communication capabilities for both military and commercial applications in the Arctic.

feed.title: The Giant Company and Instacart launch grocery delivery in 30 minutes
feed.summary: The Giant Company and Instacart have partnered to launch "Giant Instant Delivery" and "Martin’s Instant Delivery," offering grocery delivery in as fast as 30 minutes across Pennsylvania, Maryland, Virginia, and West Virginia. This service is part of Instacart’s "Convenience Hub" and competes with other rapid delivery options, including Kroger Delivery Now. The new offering aims to provide faster delivery windows and increased e-commerce convenience for shoppers, with an eye towards future growth with a new fulfillment center.

feed.title: Capitalizing on the many artificial neural network uses
feed.summary: This article explores the expanding uses of artificial neural networks (ANNs) in enterprises, highlighting their potential benefits despite challenges like ethical concerns and implementation complexities. It differentiates between convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for spatial data and recurrent neural networks (RNNs) for sequential data, providing examples such as facial recognition, medical analysis, fraud detection, and customer habit monitoring. The article also discusses crucial considerations for businesses, including addressing transparency issues, managing computational resources, and defining clear value statements before deployment.

feed.title: Best Buy to Test Return-to-Office Plans Over the Holidays
feed.summary: Best Buy will test its return-to-office plans over the holidays by asking a "small number of teams" to work from its Richfield corporate campus starting in November. This initiative will serve as a road test for the company's long-term hybrid work model, with all returning employees required to be vaccinated against Covid-19. Best Buy expects the majority of its corporate employees to return in early 2022, as many other Twin Cities companies have delayed their return-to-office plans.

feed.title: HP’s Smartest Ink Tank Printer For People That Print A Lot
feed.summary: HP Inc. has launched the new low-cost HP Smart Tank 7000 series printer, designed for individuals with high-volume printing needs. This series emphasizes sustainability, being HP's first ink tank printer made from over 25% recycled plastic, and offers smart features like self-healing Wi-Fi, automatic two-sided printing, and a spill-free refill system. The printers are available in the U.S. priced at USD$399 – $499.

feed.title: Quick on Their Fleet: Kodiak Builds Flexible, High-Performance Self-Driving Trucks on NVIDIA DRIVE
feed.summary: Kodiak Robotics has unveiled its fourth-generation self-driving truck, powered by NVIDIA DRIVE Orin. The trucks feature a lightweight mapping strategy and a discreet, modular hardware design to achieve level 4 self-driving capabilities, focusing on flexibility and live perception rather than detailed pre-constructed maps. This approach addresses the high demand for delivery and the shortage of drivers in the trucking industry.

feed.title: Amazon settles labor dispute with fired climate activists
feed.summary: Amazon has reached a private settlement with two former web designers, Emily Cunningham and Maren Costa, who were allegedly fired for their workplace activism. The National Labor Relations Board had previously accused Amazon of unfair labor practices, stating that the employees' terminations violated legal protections for advocating workplace changes related to climate change and COVID-19 safety concerns for warehouse workers. The terms of the settlement, known as a "private non-board agreement," will not be disclosed to the public but await approval from a regional director of the labor board.

feed.title: Amazon opens its first Thurston County distribution warehouse
feed.summary: Amazon has opened its first fulfillment center in Thurston County, located in Lacey, Washington. The new facility will create 1,000 jobs and is one of three warehouses on a newly developed site. This marks a significant expansion for the e-commerce giant in the state.

feed.title: Cathie Wood’s Ark Investment sells $270M worth of Tesla stake
feed.summary: Cathie Wood’s Ark Investment Management sold over 340,000 shares of Tesla, worth nearly $270 million, across three of its exchange-traded funds. This move comes as a bond selloff impacted rate-sensitive technology stocks, leading to outflows from Ark's growth-focused funds. Despite the sale, Tesla remains Ark's largest holding, with Wood predicting its shares will reach $3,000.

feed.title: PayPal’s Happy Returns partners with Staples
feed.summary: PayPal's Happy Returns has partnered with Staples to offer in-person, box-free returns at over a thousand Staples stores across the U.S., starting in California. This collaboration will make return services accessible within 10 miles for 75% of Americans and aims to streamline the return process for online shoppers while increasing foot traffic for Staples. The move follows PayPal's acquisition of Happy Returns and reflects a broader trend of payment companies engaging in acquisitions within the returns space to address growing e-commerce return challenges.

feed.title: Jabil expects to hit over $8B in net revenue in Q1 of 2022
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. (NYSE: JBL) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year, with net revenue of $7.4 billion and $29.3 billion respectively, despite supply chain challenges. Looking ahead to Q1 2022, the company projects net revenue between $8 billion and $8.6 billion, driven by its diversified business model and opportunities in vehicle electrification, connected healthcare, and infrastructure development. Jabil continues to work with major clients such as Amazon, Tesla, Apple, and Johnson & Johnson.

feed.title: General Motors' Commercial EV Unit To Expand Vehicle Lineup, Add Verizon As Customer
feed.summary: General Motors' BrightDrop electric commercial vehicle business is expanding its lineup with a second delivery van, the EV410, and has secured Verizon Communications Inc. as a customer for this new vehicle. The EV410, aimed at segments requiring smaller vehicles like grocery and telecommunications, will begin production in the second half of 2023 at GM's CAMI assembly plant in Canada. This move highlights GM's increased investment in electric and autonomous vehicles, recognizing the growing demand and financial benefits for commercial EV fleets.

feed.title: INSIGHTS | Conservative investors are pairing portfolio with principles
feed.summary: Tom Carter, a mutual fund executive, has launched the American Conservative Values ETF to allow conservative investors to align their portfolios with their principles, avoiding "woke" companies. The fund, which began in October and already manages over $28 million, aims to provide an option for individuals who wish to invest in companies that share their conservative values rather than those that engage in social and racial justice advocacy. This initiative reflects a broader movement where conservatives are using economic choices to "fight back" against companies perceived as aligning with liberal politics.

feed.title: Sonos Beam (2021, 2nd Gen) review: Compact soundbar delivers plenty of clout
feed.summary: The Sonos Beam (2nd Gen) is presented as an excellent compact soundbar that, despite sharing much of its architecture with its predecessor, notably adds virtual Dolby Atmos support, a new processor, and an updated aesthetic with a plastic grille. While it doesn't offer true Dolby Atmos like larger, pricier alternatives, its virtual surround sound experience is highly effective for its size, especially when paired with additional Sonos speakers. The soundbar also boasts a sleek design, extensive smart features including voice assistants and AirPlay 2, and numerous music streaming options, making it a versatile choice for enhancing both TV audio and general music listening.

feed.title: Aegis Baseline 10 for Joint Battle Management | Proceedings - October 2021 Vol. 147/10/1,424
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin is working with Huntington Ingalls Industries on the initial lightoff of the Aegis combat system for the USS Jack H. Lucas (DDG-125), the first Flight III Arleigh Burke-class destroyer. The new Baseline 10 software will integrate with the SPY-6 air-and-missile-defense radar, demonstrating advanced joint domain operations capabilities. Lockheed Martin is also focusing on rapid combat system upgrades through digital transformation and virtualization, aiming to deliver improvements to the fleet much faster than current timelines.

feed.title: IonQ Becomes First Publicly Traded, Pure-Play Quantum Computing Company; Closes Business Combination with dMY Technology Group III
feed.summary: IonQ, Inc. announced the completion of its business combination with dMY Technology Group, Inc. III, making it the first publicly traded, pure-play quantum computing company. The company's common stock and warrants began trading on the NYSE under "IONQ" and "IONQ.WS" respectively, after receiving $636 million in gross proceeds to fuel growth and commercialization. IonQ also reported tripling its 2021 contract bookings expectations to $15 million, highlighting the accelerating demand for quantum computing.

feed.title: Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, Disney Among Major Corporations Lying About Supporting Climate Action
feed.summary: A new analysis by watchdog group Accountable.US reveals that major corporations like Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Disney are funding lobbying groups that actively work against significant climate legislation, despite publicly claiming to support climate action. These companies are reportedly supporting or directing organizations such as the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Business Roundtable in their efforts to defeat the $3.5 trillion Build Back Better Act. This behavior is labeled as "greenwashing," where companies project an environmentally responsible image while undermining climate initiatives behind the scenes.

feed.title: Rivian details $1 billion loss, Amazon deal in IPO filing
feed.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc., an electric pickup maker backed by Amazon.com Inc., disclosed a net loss of almost $1 billion in the first half of the year in its initial public offering paperwork. The company is seeking to be valued at about $80 billion and has significant backing from investors like Amazon and Ford Motor Co., alongside strong pre-order interest for its vehicles. The filing also detailed its exclusive four-year deal with Amazon for delivery vehicles, although Amazon isn't bound by minimum purchase requirements.

feed.title: Friday’s analyst upgrades and downgrades
feed.summary: This article details various analyst upgrades and downgrades on October 1, 2021, covering companies in the fertilizer, real estate, energy infrastructure, gaming, and renewable royalties sectors. Analysts from Citi, JP Morgan, TD Securities, Credit Suisse, RBC Dominion Securities, Raymond James, National Bank Financial, CIBC World Markets, Scotia Capital, and Piper Sandler provided updated ratings, price targets, and rationales for their investment recommendations. Key companies discussed include Nutrien Ltd., various Canadian REITs, Tidewater Midstream and Infrastructure Ltd., Enthusiast Gaming Holdings Inc., Altius Renewable Royalties Corp., and several large-cap internet stocks.

feed.title: Rivian Details $1 Billion Loss, Amazon Deal in IPO Filing
feed.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc., an electric vehicle manufacturer backed by Amazon.com Inc., disclosed a net loss of almost $1 billion in the first half of the year in its initial public offering filing. The startup also revealed nearly 50,000 pre-orders for its first vehicles and committed to an exclusive four-year van deal with Amazon. The IPO filing listed a placeholder size of $100 million, which will be adjusted when the terms of the share sale are finalized.

feed.title: Amazon Turns Negative for 2021 as Higher Yields Add to Pressure
feed.summary: Amazon's stock turned negative for 2021 after a sharp decline, marking its longest slump since August 2019. The e-commerce giant's shares are being pressured by rising Treasury yields, which are negatively impacting the earnings outlook for companies with high valuations. Amazon is now the only one of Wall Street's five largest companies to be in negative territory for the year.

feed.title: U.S. Supreme Court rebuffs Oracle challenge to Pentagon cloud contract
feed.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court declined to hear Oracle Corp's challenge to the Pentagon's now-canceled $10 billion JEDI cloud computing contract. A lower court had ruled that Oracle was not harmed by any errors in the contract award process as the company would not have qualified. The JEDI contract, initially awarded to Microsoft Corp and later scrapped, is being replaced by a new contract expected to include Amazon.com.

feed.title: Cannondale bikes will be featured at new Public Lands speciality stores
feed.summary: Dick's Sporting Goods is expanding into the cycling market with its new Public Lands specialty outdoor concept stores, which will feature Cannondale mountain bikes and e-bikes, a full-service bike repair shop, and accessories. These stores aim to cater to a wide range of outdoor enthusiasts, or "explorers," offering cycling classes and events while focusing on inclusivity and supporting local outdoor communities. The first Public Lands store opened near Pittsburgh, with a second planned for Columbus, Ohio, and further brick-and-mortar expansion to be determined.

feed.title: Albertsons’ stock price has doubled since it went public. Can it keep the momentum going?
feed.summary: Albertsons' stock price has doubled since its 2020 IPO, largely due to its rapid embrace of technology and omnichannel capabilities under CEO Vivek Sankaran. The company has invested heavily in digital partnerships, pickup services, and automated micro-fulfillment centers to catch up with rivals and adapt to changing consumer behaviors. While analysts acknowledge Albertsons' significant progress in digital transformation and executive team enhancements, concerns remain about its reliance on third-party delivery and its "high-low" pricing strategy compared to competitors' everyday low prices.

feed.title: NovaBay Pharmaceuticals Agrees to Acquire Skin Care Brand DermaDoctor
feed.summary: NovaBay Pharmaceuticals has reached a definitive agreement to acquire DermaDoctor, a privately-held skin care company, for $15 million. The deal includes $12 million in cash at closing and up to $3 million in earnout payments. This acquisition is expected to double NovaBay's top-line revenue and significantly expand its presence in the U.S. beauty market, with DermaDoctor's co-founders, Audrey and Jeff Kunin, continuing in leadership roles.

feed.title: HomeGoods finally has a home online
feed.summary: HomeGoods has launched its first e-commerce offering, aiming to complement its physical stores and attract new shoppers. The online strategy for HomeGoods differs slightly from TJX's other sites like TJMaxx.com and Marshalls.com by sharing buyers and inventory due to the fast-turning SKUs in the home category, a move designed to support customer buying needs. This launch comes as the home category continues to thrive, with HomeGoods' comparable sales jumping 36 percent in the second quarter compared to 2019, despite the challenges of shipping bulky items and the ongoing debate about the profitability of e-commerce for off-price retailers.

feed.title: How Does Groupon Work and Is It Legit?
feed.summary: Groupon operates as an e-commerce platform offering discounts, limited offers, and buy-one-get-one deals by acting as a middleman between consumers and vendors. It earns commissions from sales generated through its coupons and cashback programs, which offer significant savings of 15% to 30%. Groupon is considered a legitimate and steadily growing company with few user complaints, offering value to online shoppers and those seeking local business deals.

feed.title: Korea's first-generation platforms collapse, ringing in new e-commerce era
feed.summary: First-generation South Korean e-commerce platforms like Danawa and Interpark are being sold off as they struggle to compete with newer, more innovative companies. Despite the e-commerce boom, these pioneers are succumbing to new players like Naver and Coupang, who leverage AI recommendations and fast delivery. This shift signifies a new era in the highly competitive e-commerce market in Korea.

feed.title: Mobile retail aspires to attain food truck-like popularity
feed.summary: Mobile retail is in its early stages but aims to replicate the success of food trucks by bringing shopping experiences directly to customers. Companies like Lovesac and Santa are experimenting with mobile showrooms and specialized trucks for fashion, home goods, and bike services. While some see potential for convenience and engagement, others raise concerns about scalability, profitability, and consumer perception compared to traditional retail models.

feed.title: The Beam (Gen 2) delivers in price, features and ease of use - WiFiHiFi
feed.summary: The Sonos Beam (Gen 2) soundbar offers excellent 3D sound with Dolby Atmos support at an affordable price, but fully utilizing its advanced audio features requires a newer TV with HDMI eARC and premium streaming music subscriptions. The review highlights improvements over the original Beam, such as a more powerful processor, redesigned speaker grouping, and eco-friendly packaging. It concludes that while the Beam (Gen 2) is a strong entry point into the Sonos ecosystem, the larger Sonos Arc still provides a more immersive experience.

feed.title: Once a pioneer, quantum computer developer D-Wave will start making same types of machines as rivals
feed.summary: D-Wave Systems Inc., an early leader in quantum computing with its specialized "quantum annealing" approach, announced it will now also develop general-purpose "gate-model" quantum computers, aligning with rivals like IBM and Google. This strategic shift aims to broaden the types of problems D-Wave's machines can solve, though the company remains committed to its annealing technology. The move comes after a challenging period for D-Wave, including a significant refinancing and increased competition.

feed.title: Tiger Global backs Favo, which is building an easier way for Latin Americans to order groceries online
feed.summary: Favo, a Brazilian social commerce platform for online grocery orders, raised $26.5 million in Series A funding led by Tiger Global Management, bringing its total funding to $37 million since 2019. The company enables under-employed entrepreneurs to earn commissions by facilitating last-mile grocery deliveries through local social networks. Favo plans to use the new funds for logistics, fulfillment, and expansion in Brazil and Mexico, aiming to democratize e-commerce across Latin America.

feed.title: Kroger hiring 20K associates ahead of holiday season rush
feed.summary: Kroger plans to hire 20,000 new associates across the nation to prepare for the upcoming holiday season, addressing potential labor shortages. The company is hosting a hybrid hiring event on October 13th to fill various roles including retail, e-commerce, and healthcare, offering competitive pay and benefits. Kroger is investing an additional $350 million in its associates in 2021, with average hourly wages exceeding $16 and total compensation, including benefits, over $21 an hour.

feed.title: Walmart lands Home Depot as its first fast-delivery customer
feed.summary: Walmart has secured Home Depot as the first major client for its new GoLocal fast delivery service. This partnership will focus on same-day and next-day delivery of smaller items for Home Depot, starting in select states before expanding. The collaboration highlights a growing trend of retailers working together, even competitors, to enhance e-commerce and delivery capabilities.

feed.title: Bank of America raises U.S. minimum hourly wage to $21
feed.summary: Bank of America announced on Wednesday a raise in its U.S. minimum hourly wage to $21, moving towards its promise of increasing pay to $25 an hour by 2025. This increase is part of a broader trend among major companies to boost employee wages, spurred by pandemic-fueled discussions on fair pay and competitive pressures. The bank has also urged its vendors to adopt a $15 an hour minimum wage, setting a higher standard compared to some competitors.

feed.title: GXO and Abercrombie & Fitch Co. to Open High-Tech Distribution Center in Phoenix Metropolitan Area
feed.summary: GXO Logistics and Abercrombie & Fitch Co. are partnering to open a new 715,000-square-foot distribution center in Goodyear, Arizona. This facility will serve as Abercrombie & Fitch Co.’s hub for West Coast operations, incorporating advanced automation like robotics, AI, and machine learning. The project will create over 300 jobs in the Phoenix metropolitan area, expanding GXO's regional presence and commitment to local growth.

feed.title: Pattern bags $225M; CEO says e-commerce accelerators ‘will win the day’ over aggregators
feed.summary: Pattern, an e-commerce accelerator, secured $225 million in growth funding, bringing its total valuation to $2 billion, to expand its technology and global presence. Co-founder and CEO David Wright believes accelerators, which help brands optimize sales on various marketplaces, will ultimately outperform aggregators that buy up smaller brands. The company aims to surpass $1 billion in revenue within the next year and has already partnered with over 100 brands globally.

feed.title: Discover Equal Weight Benefits With RSP
feed.summary: This article discusses the benefits of equal weight ETFs, using the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) as a prime example. It explains how equal weighting offers diversification beyond cap-weighted funds and benefits from mean-reversion, despite critics associating its outperformance with size and value factors. The article highlights RSP's long-term outperformance potential and its role in true diversification by preventing a few mega-cap stocks from dominating the index.

feed.title: Target adds 'buy now pay later' options with Affirm and Sezzle ahead of holiday shopping season
feed.summary: Target is introducing "buy now, pay later" options with Affirm and Sezzle for shoppers ahead of the 2021 holiday season, following a trend among major retailers. These installment plans offer flexible payment options, particularly appealing to younger consumers and those who may not qualify for traditional credit cards. The new services will also qualify for Target's price match guarantee, with Sezzle for smaller purchases and Affirm for purchases over $100.

feed.title: Amazon's Twitch blames configuration error for data breach
feed.summary: Amazon's Twitch has attributed a recent data breach to an error in a server configuration change, which allowed an alleged hacker to leak sensitive information. The platform is still assessing the full impact but has reset all stream keys, while assuring users that login credentials and full credit card details were not exposed. This incident follows a similar "faulty configuration change" that caused a widespread outage for Facebook Inc.

feed.title: New California-made at-home COVID test on the way, White House to spend $1B to increase supply
feed.summary: The FDA authorized the California-made Flowflex at-home COVID test, which is less expensive than other available options. The White House subsequently announced a $1 billion investment to boost the manufacturing of rapid at-home tests, aiming to quadruple supply by December to over 200 million tests per month. This move addresses high demand and concerns over test accessibility and affordability, especially as the school year progresses and holidays approach.

feed.title: Dr. Seuss Readies Grinch Holiday Licensing Program
feed.summary: Dr. Seuss Enterprises has announced an extensive Grinch holiday licensing program for the upcoming season, introducing new partners across various product categories such as apparel, accessories, pet toys, ornaments, and food. The program is expanding globally with merchandise available in Canada, the U.K., Europe, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, and Mexico. Additionally, live Grinch experiences, including the immersive Dr. Seuss Experience and "Grinchmas" celebrations at Universal Studios, will be available in the U.S.

feed.title: Target adds buy now, pay later options with Affirm, Sezzle
feed.summary: Target has partnered with Affirm and Sezzle to offer buy now, pay later options to its shoppers, in anticipation of the holiday season. This initiative aims to provide customers with more flexible and personalized payment solutions. While these options are gaining popularity, concerns exist regarding potential risks for both retailers and consumers, including missed payments and accumulating debt.

feed.title: Paul Marshall and Ian Wace’s Stock Picks: Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL) and More
feed.summary: This article details the top stock picks of hedge fund managers Paul Marshall and Ian Wace, based on their Q2 2021 holdings. It highlights a selection of companies including Biogen Inc (BIIB), Apple Inc (AAPL), Zoetis Inc. (ZTS), ViacomCBS Inc. (VIAC), and Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO), providing details on their stake value, company background, and recent analyst ratings or news. The piece also explains the investment philosophy and background of Marshall Wace LLP.

feed.title: A California investment firm went from near ruin to managing over $100 billion: Its turnaround may offer solutions to the ‘Great Resignation’
feed.summary: WCM Investment Management, a Southern California firm, rebounded from near collapse in 2011 with just $833 million in assets to managing over $100 billion by September, a tenfold increase in a decade. The firm's success is attributed to its decision to retain employees and prioritize a positive corporate culture, rather than a "toxic" one, during challenging times. This unique approach, where top management absorbed losses instead of firing staff, is highlighted as a potential lesson for companies facing the "Great Resignation."

feed.title: Affirm, Sezzle buy now-pay later options arrive at Target
feed.summary: Target has integrated buy now, pay later (BNPL) options from Sezzle and Affirm for its customers, responding to the growing trend in installment payments. Shoppers can use Affirm for online purchases over $100 and Sezzle for both online and mobile app purchases, including through Apple Pay and Google Pay. This move aims to offer greater financial flexibility to customers as BNPL services continue to gain widespread adoption, despite some concerns about potential consumer debt and credit impact.

feed.title: Bass, Berry & Sims law firm to relocate headquarters to Nashville Yards
feed.summary: Bass, Berry & Sims, one of Nashville's largest law firms, is moving its headquarters to Nashville Yards. The firm will occupy the top eight floors of a new building, following Pinnacle Financial Partners to the new development. This move signifies a significant relocation for the prominent firm within downtown Nashville.

feed.title: Shaping the Future of Delivery, Robot Serve Doesn’t Need a Delivery Man to Bring You Food
feed.summary: Serve Robotics, spun off from Uber-acquired Postmates, has developed a semi-autonomous delivery robot aimed at sustainable and accessible deliveries. These all-electric robots navigate sidewalks using cameras and Lidar sensors, with a human remote override for complex situations. The article highlights Serve's features, such as its 50 lb storage capacity and 3 mph top speed, and discusses its potential impact on delivery costs, traffic, and local restaurant sales.

feed.title: Oracle picks Joburg for its first data centre in Africa
feed.summary: Oracle has chosen Johannesburg as the site for its first data center in Africa, aiming to meet customer needs and provide cloud services within the region. This move is part of Oracle's global strategy to expand its data center footprint and support its clients in running critical workloads in the cloud. The South African market for data centers is not considered saturated, and this investment is expected to increase competition, boost cloud adoption, and foster skills development in the region.

feed.title: South Africa’s Telkom ditches Netflix
feed.summary: South African telecommunication provider Telkom SA SOC Ltd has discontinued its partnership with Netflix Inc., no longer offering the streaming service through its set-top box since October. Telkom is exploring alternative ways for its customers to access Netflix, while Netflix continues its efforts to grow its subscriber base in Africa through initiatives like a free mobile plan in Kenya and local content production. Despite the end of its Telkom partnership, Netflix maintains a presence in South Africa via a deal with MultiChoice Group Limited.

feed.title: Lithium Outlook 2022: Bracing for the Next Big Shortage
feed.summary: The article discusses the impending shortage of lithium, a critical component for green energy and electric vehicles, emphasizing that current production levels cannot meet projected demand. It highlights the contradiction in U.S. policy, where environmental conservation efforts clash with the need for increased domestic lithium mining to reduce reliance on foreign imports. The author suggests that despite vast reserves, bureaucratic hurdles and slow expansion mean the world is ill-prepared for the anticipated surge in lithium demand driven primarily by the booming EV market.

feed.title: A2C2 calls on eBay to halt the sale of airbags on its site
feed.summary: The Automotive Anti-Counterfeiting Council (A2C2) is urging eBay to stop the sale of airbags and their components on its platform, citing safety concerns over dangerous counterfeit products. While other major e-commerce sites have banned these sales, eBay continues to allow them under specific conditions, despite numerous law enforcement investigations into counterfeit airbag sales. A2C2 emphasizes the risk to both consumers and repair shops from these potentially hazardous parts.

feed.title: ClixTV Launches To Help Streamers Find Something To Watch
feed.summary: ClixTV has launched as a new streaming service designed to help viewers find content across various top subscription services, offering its own short- and long-form programming, trailers, and e-commerce opportunities. Founded by industry veterans Edward Sullivan (CEO), Stacy Jolna (CMO), and Patricia Sullivan (CCO), ClixTV aims to simplify the content search experience for consumers and provide benefits for creators, distributors, and advertisers. The platform is available on multiple devices and services including Roku, Apple TV, Amazon Fire TV, and iOS/Android.

feed.title: GitLab: Another Overpriced Tech Company
feed.summary: This article argues that GitLab (GTLB) is an overpriced tech company ahead of its IPO, with a projected valuation of $8.2 billion being significantly higher than its intrinsic value. The author highlights GitLab's unprofitable freemium model, fierce competition from profitable industry giants like Microsoft, and several red flags for investors including insider control and potentially misleading non-GAAP metrics. The analysis suggests a fair value for GitLab's stock could be as low as $5/share.

feed.title: Macy’s Taps Fanatics to Expand Licensed Sports Assortment
feed.summary: Macy's has announced a long-term partnership with Fanatics to significantly expand its licensed sports merchandise offering, allowing the department store to offer nearly 20 times more fan apparel on its website and mobile app. Fanatics will handle order fulfillment and shipping, while Macy's maintains the e-commerce front end. This collaboration aims to provide Macy's customers with a much broader selection of fan gear from various sports leagues and teams, including upscale merchandise and on-demand championship products.

feed.title: UPS, FedEx, USPS Set Holiday Shipping Dates
feed.summary: UPS, FedEx, and USPS have released their recommended holiday shipping deadlines for 2021, advising consumers to shop early due to potential supply chain issues. While some deadlines remain consistent with previous years, others have been adjusted. All three carriers are hiring a significant number of seasonal employees to manage the anticipated surge in package volume.

feed.title: Architect completes major redevelopment of West Side office tower where Amazon is a tenant
feed.summary: MdeAS, a New York architecture firm, has finished redeveloping 410 10th Ave., a 20-story office tower on Manhattan's West Side, where Amazon is a tenant. The project involved a significant renovation of the building's exterior. This redevelopment contributes to the ongoing commercial real estate activity in Midtown West.

feed.title: Amazon copied products and rigged search results to promote its own brands, documents show
authors: ADITYA KALRA in New Delhi and STEVE STECKLOW in London
feed.summary: Internal Amazon documents reportedly reveal that the e-commerce giant created knockoff goods and manipulated search results to boost its own product lines in India, despite denying such practices. This systematic campaign involved exploiting internal data to copy products and rigging search results to promote Amazon's private brands, with at least two top executives reviewing the strategy. The revelations could intensify regulatory scrutiny Amazon faces for alleged anti-competitive practices globally.

feed.title: Amazon, Third-Party Sellers Spur Fake Goods, Group Says
feed.summary: A report commissioned by the Buy Safe America Coalition claims that third-party marketplaces, particularly Amazon.com Inc., are facilitating a flood of imitation goods into the U.S. market. These counterfeit products are estimated to cost U.S. retailers approximately $54 billion annually in lost sales, with the majority originating from China. The findings coincide with a congressional hearing on an online marketplace bill.

feed.title: Amazon Seeks Used Long-Range Cargo Jets
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is reportedly looking to acquire used long-range Boeing 777-300ER and Airbus A330-300 cargo planes, signaling its intent to expand its cross-border shipping capabilities and compete more directly with UPS and FedEx. The move would allow Amazon to directly import products from countries like China and further reduce its reliance on third-party carriers for its rapidly expanding Amazon Air fleet.

feed.title: How Chipotle feels about spending $2 billion a year on labor
feed.summary: Chipotle invests approximately $2 billion annually in its labor force, viewing it as a long-term return on investment rather than a mere cost, especially amidst current labor shortages. The company emphasizes benefits like debt-free education and career progression to attract and retain talent, aligning with its "people-first" philosophy and appealing to ESG-conscious consumers. This strategy, though difficult for Wall Street to model precisely, has contributed to strong market performance and positions Chipotle to better manage labor market pressures.

feed.title: Emerging trends in IoT gateway and edge application management in a cloud native paradigm
feed.summary: This article discusses the emerging trends in IoT gateway and edge application management within a cloud-native paradigm. It highlights the shift towards remote, cloud-based management due to digital transformation, especially post-pandemic, and addresses the security and architectural challenges this presents. The author outlines six critical pillars for cloud-native IoT management in hybrid and multi-cloud environments, such as compute scalability and robust data processing, and introduces Cisco's IoT Operations Dashboard as a solution.

feed.title: GXO seeks to hire 9,000 workers ahead of peak season
feed.summary: GXO Logistics, Inc. plans to hire 9,000 workers in North America and 20,000 globally to prepare for the upcoming holiday peak season. This hiring surge is coupled with a 40% year-over-year increase in investment in warehouse automation technology, including the deployment of 3,100 robots in North America by year-end. The move addresses a challenging labor market with high job openings while aiming to boost productivity and improve employee experience through automation.

feed.title: How ‘Squid Game’ reveals the flaw in Netflix’s products strategy
feed.summary: Netflix's new partnership with Walmart for a digital storefront, the Netflix Hub, is criticized for its delayed inclusion of merchandise from the hugely popular show "Squid Game." While Netflix aims to diversify revenue through licensed products, the article highlights that fans are more interested in "real" items from the show, like Vans sneakers and tracksuits, which are experiencing massive sales spikes independently, revealing a flaw in Netflix's product strategy. The company's own official merchandise for "Squid Game" is described as limited and generic, not capitalizing on the immediate demand for authentic-looking items from the show.

feed.title: The Proshares Ultra Short ETF (SQQQ) Stock Is Up 11.8% in One Month. What’s Next?
feed.summary: The ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ ETF (SQQQ) surged 11.8% over the past month as the Nasdaq index faced a broad sell-off in tech and growth stocks. However, the author views SQQQ as a high-risk, short-term investment, expecting its rally to be temporary given the Nasdaq's long-term upward trend and the ETF's historical performance during market crashes. Most retail investors are advised to avoid this inverse ETF due to features like time decay and high fees.

feed.title: Nike, Air Jordan And Lululemon Stand Out In Cowen’s Gen Z/Millennial Survey
feed.summary: A Cowen survey reveals that Nike, Air Jordan, and Lululemon are top brands among Gen Z and Millennials in the active lifestyle space. The survey also highlighted Amazon's dominance in shopping preferences, the increasing importance of ESG and social impact in purchasing decisions, and the growth of social commerce and resale markets among younger demographics.

feed.title: SATELLITE 2021 Wrap-Up: Dreams in the Sky Need a New Reality on the Ground
feed.summary: The SATELLITE 2021 conference highlighted the critical importance of ground segment technology in the satellite industry, advocating for increased collaboration and open standards among ground players and operators. Industry leaders like Steve Collar (SES), Kevin Steen (ST Engineering iDirect), and Samer Halawi (Intelsat) emphasized the need for a shift towards software-defined, virtualized environments to break away from proprietary hardware, reduce costs, and integrate with mainstream telecommunications, especially with the surge in 5G and multi-orbit initiatives. While there's optimism and investment, the consensus was that a revolution on the ground, mirroring advancements in space, is essential for achieving global connectivity and supporting increasingly complex satellite networks.

feed.title: Reliance tops India Inc in World's Best Employer rankings 2021. ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank and HCL also in the Forbes’ top 100 list
feed.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd (RIL) has been ranked as the top Indian company in Forbes' World's Best Employers rankings 2021, placing 52nd overall. Other Indian companies in the top 100 include ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, and HCL Technologies. The rankings are based on a survey of 150,000 workers globally who rated their employers on various aspects like image, talent development, and social responsibility.

feed.title: Pardon the Disruption: Kroger is showcasing its range with Ocado
feed.summary: Kroger's partnership with Ocado, initially met with skepticism, is now proving prescient due to the pandemic-driven e-commerce boom. The recent expansion to 16 fulfillment centers showcases a flexible, multi-pronged approach, incorporating not just large customer fulfillment centers (CFCs) but also smaller facilities and micro-fulfillment sites for speedier service. This strategy targets diverse consumer demands for online grocery convenience and allows Kroger to aggressively enter new markets like the Northeast, despite the high capital expenditures involved.

feed.title: NXP selects AWS to host chip design & verification systems
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors has chosen AWS as its primary cloud provider for electronic design automation (EDA) workloads, migrating the majority of its EDA systems to AWS infrastructure. This move aims to leverage AWS's scalable compute power to accelerate chip design and verification processes, reduce costs, and minimize scheduling risks. The partnership expands on previous collaborations between NXP and AWS, focusing on accelerating semiconductor innovation and bringing new designs to market faster.

feed.title: How Stephen Garden took Onica from class assignment to a multi-million dollar acquisition in just 6 years
feed.summary: Stephen Garden transformed Onica from a class assignment at London Business School into a multi-million dollar acquisition by Rackspace in just six years by focusing on Amazon Web Services expertise. Despite Onica's rapid success and global reach, Garden stepped down from his SVP role at Rackspace in 2020 to prioritize personal well-being, taking a year-long sabbatical. The article highlights the intense dedication required for such entrepreneurial success and the importance of finding life balance.

The article discusses the growing trend of enormous special stock awards for startup founders, often far exceeding compensation for CEOs of established companies. It highlights examples like Archer Aviation, Palantir Technologies, and GoodRx Holdings, whose founders received nine-figure packages even before their companies were profitable or generated revenue. This practice contrasts with earlier Silicon Valley norms where founders like Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos relied on the appreciation of their initial equity, and it raises concerns about shareholder dilution and corporate governance.

feed.title: The Coffee Sector Reflects Marketplace Trends
feed.summary: The coffee sector is undergoing significant changes driven by consumer demand for sustainability, convenience, and variety. Grocers are responding by offering more better-for-you and fortified products, sustainably sourced options, and convenient ready-to-drink and single-serve formats. In-store coffee bars and partnerships with local roasters also continue to be popular attractions.

feed.title: Amazon plans to hire 150,000 for holiday, 50% more than in 2020
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to hire 150,000 seasonal workers for the upcoming holiday shopping period, marking a 50% increase from the previous year. This hiring surge is part of the company's continuous expansion of its logistics operations, with new facilities opening regularly across the U.S. To attract and retain these workers amid intense competition, Amazon is offering an average starting pay of $18 an hour, along with signing bonuses up to $3,000 and additional incentives for overnight or weekend shifts.

feed.title: As it embarks on nationwide hiring spree, Amazon opens huge shipping facility in Austin area
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has officially opened a 3.8-million-square-foot fulfillment center in Pflugerville, expanding its Central Texas presence. This new facility marks the beginning of a significant local and nationwide hiring push, with an additional 3,000 seasonal jobs anticipated in Austin this year. The investment underscores Amazon's continued growth and demand for labor in the region.

feed.title: Alibaba unveils custom ARM-based server chip for cloud computing data centers
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd has developed its own ARM-based server chip, the Yitian 710, for use in its data centers, signifying its continued push into semiconductor development. This move aligns with similar efforts by other global cloud providers like Amazon and Huawei, and supports the Chinese government's initiative to bolster its domestic chip industry. The chip, developed by Alibaba's T-Head unit, will not be commercially available outside the company, which is China's largest cloud computing provider.

feed.title: Wheels Up Announces Srikanth Satya As Chief Technology And Development Officer
feed.summary: Wheels Up has announced the appointment of Srikanth Satya as its new Chief Technology and Development Officer. Satya brings over two decades of technology leadership experience from companies like Amazon, Microsoft, and Dell EMC, at a time when Wheels Up is accelerating its digital and marketplace transformation. His role will be crucial in developing innovative technologies and products to enhance customer experience and drive the company's growth strategy in private aviation.

feed.title: REPORT: Amazon.com partnering with Puyallup Tribe to Build Sorting Center on Tribal Lands near Tacoma, Wash.
feed.summary: Amazon.com is collaborating with the Puyallup Tribe of Indians to establish a large sorting center on tribal lands near Tacoma, Washington. This 520,000-square-foot facility is expected to create over 500 jobs, with the tribe actively seeking employment opportunities for its members. The partnership includes Amazon's support for the Puyallup Tribe's Tribal Employment Rights Ordinance.

feed.title: Comcast, Walmart team up to sell smart TVs with the media firm's software
feed.summary: Comcast has partnered with Walmart to launch XClass TV, a new line of smart TVs that will run on Comcast's operating system. These TVs, manufactured by Hisense, will be sold at Walmart stores and online, integrating streaming, on-demand, broadcast, and cable content with voice search capabilities. This move aims to strengthen Comcast's position in the competitive streaming market, which is currently dominated by companies like Roku and Amazon's Fire TV.

feed.title: Equinix plans two new Sydney data centres through $775M joint venture
feed.summary: Equinix announced a $775 million (US$575 million) joint venture with PGIM Real Estate to construct two new xScale data centers, SY9x and SY10x, in Sydney. These facilities will offer over 55 megawatts of power capacity, catering to hyperscale companies and public cloud providers like AWS and Microsoft Azure. The first data center, SY9x, is expected to open in Q1 2022, with PGIM Real Estate holding an 80% equity interest and Equinix the remaining 20% in the venture.

feed.title: Carbon Capture Inc. Closes $35 Million Series A Funding From Prime Movers Lab, Rio Tinto, Idealab Studio, and Time Ventures
feed.summary: Carbon Capture Inc. (CarbonCapture), a climate tech company specializing in direct air capture (DAC) technology, announced it closed a $35 million Series A funding round and appointed Adrian Corless as CEO. The funding, led by Prime Movers Lab with participation from Rio Tinto and Time Ventures, will accelerate product development and initial deployments of its DAC systems across the U.S. Notably, the company’s technology utilizes zeolites, which are inexpensive, environmentally friendly, and already produced at an industrial scale, to capture atmospheric CO2, and this offers a scalable solution for carbon removal efforts.

feed.title: Economic impact for small businesses, powered by partnership with Amazon
feed.summary: Amazon's 2021 Small Business Empowerment Report highlights the success of its two-decade-old strategy of embracing third-party sellers, which has benefited both customers and small businesses. Products from small and medium-sized businesses now account for over half of everything sold in Amazon's store, with the company investing more than $18 billion in seller success last year. Amazon's investments and programs have helped sellers launch, scale, and build their brands, leading to the creation of an estimated 1.8 million U.S.-based jobs.

feed.title: Judge Dismisses Amazon Class Action Lawsuit Over Video Ownership
feed.summary: A California judge has dismissed a class action lawsuit against Amazon regarding video ownership, ruling that the plaintiff lacked standing to sue. Amanda Caudel alleged that Amazon could revoke access to purchased videos, but her access to all 36 videos she bought remained available. The lawsuit claimed Amazon misled customers about the permanency of "purchased" digital content compared to rented content for a higher price.

feed.title: Alibaba unveils ‘breakthrough’ chip for its servers, cloud computing
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding has debuted its new Yitian 710 CPU, an internally designed chip for its Panjiu servers and cloud computing operations, at its annual Apsara developer conference. This development signifies a move towards greater semiconductor self-reliance for China and is based on Arm's architecture. The chip, which is not for commercial sale, is expected to enhance Alibaba's computing capabilities.

feed.title: SpaceX could make Elon Musk world’s first trillionaire, says Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analysts predict that Elon Musk's space exploration company, SpaceX, could elevate him to the status of the world's first trillionaire. While much of his current wealth comes from Tesla, analysts believe SpaceX's advancements in rockets and space infrastructure, including its Starship reusable rockets, position it for significant growth and potentially make it the most highly valued company globally. SpaceX is currently valued at an estimated $200 billion and is expected to rise further as it expands into various industries.

feed.title: The Strategy to Build Secure Multi-cloud Networking
feed.summary: This article discusses the strategy for building secure multi-cloud networking, emphasizing that successful multi-cloud adoption requires a network architecture that facilitates application distribution while maintaining security, configuration policies, and performance. It highlights the benefits of a multi-cloud approach, such as service choice, reduced vendor lock-in, low latency, and improved disaster recovery, while also addressing common challenges like complex management and inconsistent security policies. The paper proposes an application-aware, cloud-ready network architecture with software-defined networking as a core component to achieve efficient and secure multi-cloud environments.

feed.title: Protesters denounce Netflix over Chappelle transgender comments
feed.summary: Around 100 people protested outside Netflix Inc.'s headquarters in Los Angeles against the streaming company's decision to release Dave Chappelle's new comedy special, which they claim ridicules transgender people. Netflix employees, transgender rights advocates, and public officials participated in the demonstration, raising concerns about the content and sparking a discussion about the line between edgy jokes and harmful speech. This protest follows Netflix's Chief Content Officer Ted Sarandos acknowledging missteps in his communication regarding the special.

feed.title: Amazon Staten Island Warehouse Workers Lead Push to Unionize
feed.summary: Over 2,000 workers at four Amazon.com Inc. facilities in Staten Island have signed a petition to federal labor officials to hold an election to form the Amazon Labor Union. This latest effort follows a failed unionization attempt in Bessemer, Alabama, and comes as labor organizers hope to capitalize on growing public support for essential workers. The union aims to secure higher wages, job security, and better working conditions for Amazon employees.

feed.title: One Woman’s Rise to Amazon Director Is Model for Board Diversity
feed.summary: Edith Cooper's recent appointments to the boards of PepsiCo Inc. and Amazon.com Inc. highlight progress in board diversity for women and people of color. Her board changes, along with others, contributed to women gaining 13 net seats on S&P 500 boards in September. Cooper emphasizes that while progress has been made, there is still a significant journey towards achieving parity in power.

feed.title: Trump wants his media company to rival Twitter, Netflix and Amazon
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump announced the launch of Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG), aiming to create a media network that rivals Big Tech platforms like Twitter, Netflix, and Amazon. The company plans to launch a social media site called TRUTH Social, develop a subscription video-on-demand service (TMTG+), and build internet technology infrastructure. This initiative comes after Trump's bans from major social media platforms and saw TMTG's stock surge over 350% on its debut through a merger with Digital World Acquisition Corp.

feed.title: GEP doubles global partner network of consulting and technology firms
feed.summary: GEP, a supply chain software and consulting firm, has expanded its Partner United program by 114% since January 2021, doubling its global network of consulting and technology firms. This growth is driven by increasing client demand for digitally optimized supply chains, with new partners like Optis Consulting, Synergy Partners, Stratesys, Dun & Bradstreet, Pagero, and TealBook joining the initiative. The program aims to leverage diverse industry expertise and regional specializations to deliver immediate results to clients facing global supply chain challenges.

feed.title: Aruba Launches Network Switch Armed with Pensando Programmable Chip
feed.summary: Aruba has partnered with silicon startup Pensando to launch the CX 10000 Series network switch, integrating Pensando's Elba DPU to provide various network services as distributed functions. This new switch aims to help enterprises manage east-west traffic more efficiently and cost-effectively, bringing hyperscaler networking capabilities to their own data centers. It promises significant improvements in performance and scalability by offloading tasks typically handled by separate appliances directly into the network fabric.

feed.title: Navitas begins trading on Nasdaq as NVTS debuts after seven years as start-up
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor, a gallium nitride (GaN) power integrated circuit firm, has begun trading its common shares and warrants on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbols ‘NVTS’ and ‘NVTSW’. Founded in 2014, Navitas introduced what it claims are the first commercial GaN power integrated circuits, which offer faster charging and more power in smaller packages compared to silicon chips. The company aims to expand into consumer, solar, energy storage, data center, and electric vehicle markets, projecting significant reductions in electricity costs and CO2 emissions.

feed.title: What’s Happening With Clean Energy Fuels Stock?
feed.summary: Clean Energy Fuels stock has seen recent gains due to new contract wins, including deals for a hydrogen fueling station and liquid natural gas supply for container ships, leading to a 15% rise over the last month. Despite past mixed performance and unprofitability, the company's focus on renewable natural gas (RNG) and favorable regulatory and corporate interest in climate change initiatives position it for future growth. The company aims for 100% RNG in its fuel mix within five years, attracting major deals with companies like Total, BP, and Amazon.

feed.title: WD Blue SN570 Gen3 NVMe SSD Review – Performance and Value in a DRAM-less SSD
feed.summary: This review examines the WD Blue SN570 Gen3 NVMe SSD, highlighting its performance and value despite being a DRAM-less drive. It draws comparisons to the Samsung 980, another high-performing DRAM-less SSD, and emphasizes that typical users will not notice a performance difference in everyday use. The article details the SSD's specifications, including its proprietary controller, single NAND chip, various capacities, speeds, endurance, and competitive pricing.

feed.title: Amazon warehouse workers in New York file for a union vote
feed.summary: Amazon warehouse workers in Staten Island, New York, have taken a formal step towards unionization, submitting approximately 2,000 signatures for a union election. This grassroots effort is led by the Amazon Labor Union, an independent worker group formed by Chris Smalls, who was fired after leading a pandemic walkout. The organizers are seeking better working conditions, higher wages, and improved benefits for the estimated 7,000 employees across four facilities.

feed.title: What to Expect Ahead of Amazon's (AMZN) Q3 Earnings Release?
feed.summary: Amazon (AMZN) is set to release its Q3 2021 earnings on October 28, with net sales projected to be between $106 billion and $112 billion, and the Zacks Consensus Estimate for EPS at $8.72, a decline from the prior year. The company's performance is expected to be driven by its Prime, retail, and streaming services, and the strength of its AWS portfolio and smart device offerings. However, accelerating coronavirus-related expenses might impact the third-quarter results.

feed.title: The 10 Best Satellite Phones for World Travel
feed.summary: This article reviews the 10 best satellite phones for world travel, highlighting their features, connectivity, and suitability for various remote environments. It covers devices from brands like BlueCosmo, Garmin, ZOLEO, Spot, and Thuraya, offering options for different needs including global coverage, smartphone integration, and budget-friendliness. The piece emphasizes the importance of satellite phones for emergency communication in areas without cellular service.

feed.title: Redwire to provide 3D printing for Blue Origin's Orbital Reef space station
feed.summary: Redwire Space will provide 3D printing capabilities for Orbital Reef, a new commercially developed space station by Blue Origin and Sierra Space. This station aims to open new markets in space by offering a "mixed-use business park" in low Earth orbit, facilitating research, manufacturing, and tourism. Redwire's expertise in in-space additive manufacturing will be crucial for building habitats and supporting activities on Orbital Reef, which is expected to begin operating in the second half of this decade.

feed.title: Amazon Bulks Up Shipping Capacity to Battle Holiday Season Snarls
feed.summary: Amazon announced it has doubled its container processing capacity and secured more shipping storage from ocean freight carriers to mitigate supply chain issues ahead of the holiday shopping season. The company also plans to hire 150,000 seasonal workers and has increased its ports of entry by 50%. This move comes as other major retailers like Walmart and Target are also chartering ships to cope with pandemic-driven supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: Cub Foods Ramps Up Delivery Service
feed.summary: Cub Foods has partnered with Capstone Logistics to expand its grocery and liquor delivery service in Minnesota. Capstone Logistics will deploy 300 independent contributors to deliver items from over 80 Cub stores and 27 wine and liquor stores, utilizing its "MileZero" platform for optimized route planning and real-time delivery updates. This collaboration aims to enhance Cub's online shopping experience, aligning with the growing demand for convenient online grocery services.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc. opening 16th distribution facility, at building just east of downtown
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is opening its 16th distribution facility, a "last-mile" delivery station, just east of downtown. This new facility on Lebanon Pike is crucial for accelerating deliveries for Amazon's same-day or Prime services.

feed.title: Navitas Semiconductor Goes Public With $1.7B Valuation
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor went public on October 20 under the symbol "NVTS" with a market value of roughly $2 billion, having completed a reverse merger with Live Oak Acquisition Corp. II. The company, which develops gallium nitride semiconductors, expects $27 million in revenue for 2021 and anticipates its revenue to more than double next year with an influx of $320 million from going public, allowing expansion into data centers, solar, and electric vehicles. Co-founders Gene Sheridan and Dan Kinzer celebrated the IPO seven years after starting the El Segundo-based company.

feed.title: BD Begins Shipments Of First Smartphone Interpreted Over-The-Counter Rapid COVID-19 Test
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has started shipping its first over-the-counter rapid COVID-19 antigen test that uses smartphone computer vision technology to interpret results. The BD Veritor™ At-Home COVID-19 Test is now available on Amazon.com and addresses common at-home testing challenges with definitive digital results, step-by-step app instructions, and automated reporting to public health agencies. This test aims to make COVID-19 testing faster, easier, and more reliable for at-home use.

feed.title: Silicon Valley Battery Veteran Ronnie Tao Joins Amprius Technologies As Vice President Of Business Development
feed.summary: Amprius Technologies, Inc. announced the appointment of Ronnie Tao as Vice President of Business Development. Tao brings extensive experience from companies like Sony Electronics and Amazon, and will focus on expanding Amprius's market reach for its high-energy density lithium-ion batteries. This strategic hire aims to capitalize on increasing commercial opportunities for their Silicon Nanowire anode battery platform.

feed.title: Amazon Verizon 5G LEO Expansion Claims Altruistic Aim
feed.summary: Amazon's Project Kuiper and Verizon are expanding their partnership to combine Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network with Verizon's terrestrial mobile network. They aim to expand internet access to unserved and underserved communities in the USA, delivering "cellular backhaul solutions" for Verizon's 5G and 4G LTE networks. While they claim an altruistic goal of making technology "accessible for all" and "fast, affordable broadband," the companies have not indicated that the service will be free.

feed.title: Blue Origin, led by Jeff Bezos, reveals the concept of the commercial space station 'Orbital Reef'
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin, in partnership with Sierra Space, announced plans for a commercial space station called "Orbital Reef." This station is intended for various commercial uses, including research, tourism, and film production, with operations aimed to begin in the latter half of the 2020s. Orbital Reef is also supported by Boeing, Redwire Space, Genesis Engineering Solutions, and Arizona State University, and is expected to provide end-to-end services for space travel beginners and foster a new ecosystem in space.

feed.title: Big Retailers Becoming Big Property Owners With Warehouse Deals - WSJ
feed.summary: Major retailers such as Amazon and Costco are increasingly opting to own their warehouses rather than lease them. This strategy aims to boost e-commerce operations, save long-term costs, and gain more control over their logistics. It signifies a growing trend of retailers investing in commercial real estate's burgeoning warehouse sector.

feed.title: FedEx Express introduces Reusable Packaging Portfolio
feed.summary: FedEx Express has launched its Reusable Pak in Europe, a new sustainable packaging option for eCommerce shipments. This initiative responds to growing consumer demand for sustainable packaging and aims to reduce waste by allowing packages to be resealed for returns. The company is committed to integrating sustainability into its operations, working towards carbon-neutral operations by 2040.

feed.title: Saks E-Commerce Business Pairs Robots With Workers
feed.summary: Saks's stand-alone e-commerce unit has started using a high-tech fulfillment center in Pennsylvania, deploying autonomous robots to assist workers with online orders. This move, operated by GXO Logistics Inc., aims to manage the significant increase in online sales. The initiative seeks to capture the holiday rush by pairing robots with human workers to efficiently locate luxury items.

feed.title: Opinion | Tesla Is Worth $1 Trillion
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. has achieved a market capitalization exceeding $1 trillion, a significant milestone driven recently by a large order from Hertz for 100,000 Model 3 cars. This makes Tesla's valuation higher than the next nine largest automakers combined, demonstrating Elon Musk's success as a market disrupter. Despite this success, the article questions why the company still relies on taxpayer subsidies.

feed.title: Walmart Adds Bill Payment Service VanillaDirect to Stores
feed.summary: Walmart is partnering with InComm Payments to introduce the VanillaDirect bill payment service in its 4,700 U.S. stores. This new service allows customers to pay bills with cash and fund prepaid accounts, providing access to over 15,000 billers from various sectors like utilities, telecommunications, and banking. This initiative aims to offer a convenient solution for cash-centric customers.

feed.title: You can finally buy rapid at-home COVID-19 tests on Amazon
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson and Co. (BD) has started selling its rapid Veritor COVID-19 antigen test on Amazon, joining Intrivo’s On/Go test. Both tests utilize an app for guidance and aim to make at-home testing more accessible and user-friendly. These developments, along with recent FDA approvals and government initiatives, are expected to increase the availability of affordable rapid tests to help control the pandemic.

feed.title: Genius Brands Announces Transformational Acquisition of WOW! Unlimited Media Inc., Creating a Global Animation & Digital Media Powerhouse
feed.summary: Genius Brands International, Inc. announced its acquisition of WOW! Unlimited Media Inc. for approximately US$53 million in cash and stock, creating a global animation and digital media powerhouse. This acquisition is expected to grow Genius Brands' pro-forma revenues by over 1100%, add a robust pipeline and over $62 million in secured future revenue from top clients, and expand its audience into teens and young adults through WOW!'s extensive social media footprint, including the Channel Frederator Network. Michael Hirsh, CEO of WOW!, will join the Genius Brands Board of Directors, and the deal is anticipated to generate significant financial benefits and synergies through new IP monetization and cost savings.

feed.title: General Mills homes in on kids’ constipation with Doolies start-up brand
feed.summary: General Mills is launching a new start-up brand called Doolies, focusing on high-fibre snacks, bites, and smoothies to address children's constipation. This initiative is part of the company's Accelerate Strategy, aiming for sustained organic sales growth through in-house innovation. Doolies products were initially sold direct-to-consumer and will launch on Amazon in November, with potential for a broader retail rollout next year.

feed.title: Electrofuels Innovator Infinium Announces $69M in New Funding to Accelerate Production of Groundbreaking Ultra-Low Carbon Fuels for the Transportation Industry
feed.summary: Infinium, a leader in electrofuels production, announced it has closed a $69 million funding round led by Amazon's Climate Pledge Fund and NextEra Energy Resources, LLC. This funding will accelerate the production of ultra-low carbon electrofuels, which can seamlessly replace traditional jet fuel and diesel in existing transportation infrastructure. The investment aims to help decarbonize the transportation sector and meet critical carbon reduction goals for companies like Amazon.

feed.title: Verizon + Amazon’s Project Kuiper Now Involved In A Strategic Collaboration
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. and Amazon’s Project Kuiper have formed a strategic collaboration to provide connectivity solutions to unserved and underserved communities globally. This partnership aims to combine Project Kuiper's LEO satellite system with Verizon's wireless technology to expand data networks and offer new connectivity solutions across various industries. Both companies are working together to define technical requirements and accelerate the benefits of wireless connectivity for a wider range of customers.

feed.title: Here's what will take Whole Foods' old space on North Dale Mabry
feed.summary: Burlington, a big-box discount retailer, is set to occupy the former Whole Foods location on North Dale Mabry in Tampa. Whole Foods moved to Midtown, opening up the space for Burlington, which plans to renovate the real estate. The details of the new tenant were confirmed through city documents.

feed.title: Celsius is luring consumers that don’t want ‘grandfather’s energy drink,’ CEO says
feed.summary: Celsius is experiencing rapid growth by targeting consumers who prefer healthier alternatives to traditional energy drinks like Red Bull and Monster. Despite pandemic challenges, the company adapted by shifting to online sales and expanding retail presence, leading to significant sales increases. CEO John Fieldly notes that Celsius's appeal to a younger demographic and its focus on functional ingredients are driving its market share expansion in the growing energy drink sector.

feed.title: Peyton Manning latest celebrity Fanatics investor; J&J Vision revenue rises
feed.summary: Former NFL star Peyton Manning is among new celebrity investors in Candy Digital, an NFT company majority-owned by Jacksonville-based Fanatics. Candy Digital recently raised $100 million, valuing it at $1.5 billion, while Fanatics itself is valued at $18 billion. Separately, Johnson & Johnson Vision reported a 10.1% increase in third-quarter revenue, reaching $1.19 billion, driven by international sales and surgical products despite a decline in U.S. contact lens sales.

feed.title: A sea of opportunities in ASEAN’s post-pandemic future
feed.summary: Despite short-term negative investor sentiment due to recurrent COVID-19 waves, ASEAN's long-term fundamentals remain strong. The region is poised for growth through enhanced regional integration, a booming digital economy, and increasing focus on green infrastructure. These factors suggest a promising post-pandemic recovery and new investment opportunities.

feed.title: Apple and Amazon Stock Dives Set to Erase $200 Billion in Value
feed.summary: Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN) are expected to see their combined market valuation drop by over $200 billion when markets open Friday after both companies reported disappointing earnings. Apple's revenue missed expectations due to supply constraints, while Amazon's revenue forecast was below consensus and it warned of high costs impacting holiday quarter profits.

feed.title: Invesco ESG NASDAQ 100 ETF
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive guide to the Invesco ESG NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQMG), detailing its profile, price, dividend, valuation, expense ratio, holdings, and performance metrics. It includes vital information like its inception date, index tracked, assets under management, and comparisons to alternative ETFs. The report also highlights the ETF's ESG themes and scores, geographical exposure, sector allocation, and volatility analysis.

feed.title: Amazon reveals 20% stake in electric-vehicle maker Rivian
feed.summary: Amazon disclosed a 20% ownership stake in electric-vehicle maker Rivian Automotive as of September 30, 2021, with a carrying value of $3.8 billion. The disclosure came ahead of Rivian's expected IPO in November, highlighting the close ties between the two companies. Amazon has contracted Rivian to produce 100,000 electric last-mile delivery vehicles by 2030, with 10,000 expected on the road by next year.

feed.title: Amazon Stake in Rivian Disclosed as 20%
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has disclosed a 20% ownership stake in electric vehicle maker Rivian Automotive Inc., with a carrying value of $3.8 billion as of September 30. This investment highlights Amazon's significant commitment to Rivian, with an order placed for 100,000 battery-powered delivery vans. Rivian, also backed by Ford Motor Co., is preparing for an IPO with an $80 billion valuation target, despite an anticipated quarterly loss as it ramps up production of its R1T pickup.

feed.title: Amazon discloses 20% stake in electric vehicle maker Rivian ahead of IPO
feed.summary: Amazon has revealed a 20% ownership stake in electric-vehicle manufacturer Rivian Automotive Inc., valued at $3.8 billion as of September 30. This disclosure comes as Rivian prepares for an IPO and has an order from Amazon for 100,000 battery-powered delivery vans. The investment highlights Amazon's commitment to curbing its greenhouse gas emissions and positions Rivian as a significant player in the EV market.

feed.title: Move over Apple, Microsoft now the world’s most valuable company
feed.summary: Microsoft Corp. surpassed Apple Inc. to become the world's most valuable public company after Apple's shares fell due to a $6 billion sales hit from global supply chain issues. Microsoft's stock has surged over 45% this year driven by cloud-based services demand, while Apple, despite robust share buybacks, saw its market capitalization slip below Microsoft's. Both companies face ongoing supply chain challenges, though Microsoft anticipates a strong end to the year for its cloud business.

feed.title: TIDLOR augments visibility and protection for customer data and applications across public cloud IaaS
feed.summary: Ngern Tid Lor (TIDLOR), a leading Thai microfinance company, partnered with Palo Alto Networks to enhance the security of its public cloud infrastructure. Facing the need for improved application-level visibility and protection against cyber threats in the BFSI sector, TIDLOR implemented Palo Alto Networks' VM-Series Virtual Next-Generation Firewall. This solution led to increased visibility, optimized security, prevented breaches, automated endpoint detection, and significantly reduced staff time spent on monitoring and remediation.

feed.title: Cutting Carbon Emissions Remains a Struggle for Shipping Industry
feed.summary: The shipping industry is facing significant challenges in reducing carbon emissions due to limited supplies and high costs of cleaner fuel alternatives. Despite pressure from governments and major companies like Amazon and IKEA for stricter zero-carbon targets, a lack of consensus on future fuels and the immense investment required ($3 trillion) are hindering the transition. While some companies like Maersk are investing in dual-fuel vessels, the majority of new ship orders still rely on conventional heavy oil or liquefied natural gas, highlighting the industry's dilemma.

feed.title: Abercrombie & Fitch customers can now get same-day delivery in CT
feed.summary: Abercrombie & Fitch is launching a same-day delivery service across all its U.S. stores, including locations in Connecticut, for a $10 fee. This initiative is a permanent effort to serve customers better and is not a response to current staffing or supply chain issues. The retailer has partnered with several delivery services to offer this option, which is seen as a strategic move to reposition the brand and provide convenience, especially heading into the holiday season.

feed.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger’s “insane” plan to restore the company’s glory days
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger is implementing an "insane" and ambitious plan to regain the company's market leadership. This involves massive capital expenditures between $25 billion and $28 billion annually, along with $15 billion in R&D, to improve technology and capacity, especially in foundry business. The strategy aims for performance parity by 2024 and industry leadership by 2025, a bold move given Intel's recent struggles against competitors like Apple and ARM-based chips.
