feed.title: Chip makers are threatening to scrap future US factories without generous tax breaks
feed.summary: Major semiconductor manufacturers like Intel, Samsung, and TSMC are threatening to abandon plans for new US factories unless they receive significant government subsidies and tax breaks. While these companies claim the incentives are critical for their investments, experts suggest they are leveraging national security concerns and the current chip shortage to secure public funding for facilities they would likely build anyway due to the US's skilled workforce and specialized equipment manufacturers. The article highlights concerns about a potential "bullwhip effect" in the semiconductor market, where a current shortage could be followed by a glut once new factories come online.

feed.title: Deloitte, DXC, TCS and specialist firms bag AWS partner awards
feed.summary: Deloitte, DXC Technology, and Tata Consultancy Services, along with specialist firms Itoc and Intellify, have received AWS partner awards in Australia and New Zealand. Deloitte was named AWS Global Systems Integrator Partner of the Year for its significant investment in AWS expertise and innovation. Itoc won Australian Consulting Partner of the Year, while Intellify secured AWS Data, Analytics, and Machine Learning Partner of the Year.

feed.title: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge Eyeing Up to $707 Million IPO Valuation
feed.summary: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge Holdings Inc., a Chico, Calif.-based digital native fashion brand, is preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) with a valuation between $595 million and $706.6 million. The company plans to sell 5.8 million shares at $16 to $19 each, intending to use the generated funds to pay off a term loan and redeem preferred stock. Despite a dip in revenues in 2020 due to the pandemic, Lulu's rebounded in the first half of the current year, leveraging a "test, learn and reorder" strategy for its affordable luxury women's fashion.

feed.title: Massive Central Texas spread goes for $240 million
feed.summary: A North Texas-based investment group has acquired the 31,000-acre Sandow Lakes Ranch in Central Texas from Alcoa Corp. for $240 million. This property, spanning Milam and Lee counties, was formerly an industrial site for an aluminum smelter and coal mining but has been restored with lakes and farmland. The sale was brokered by Dallas property broker Bernard Uechtritz, marking one of his most significant and complex transactions.

feed.title: What could disrupt Amazon?
feed.summary: This article explores potential disruptors for Amazon, analyzing several scenarios ranging from government intervention and environmental policies to technological advancements and cultural shifts. Author David Moschella, a senior IT and Innovation Foundation Fellow, outlines seven key scenarios, emphasizing AWS's critical role and the differences between Amazon and rivals like Microsoft. The piece highlights how traditional tech dominance patterns are evolving, prompting a wider consideration of disruption sources.

feed.title: List of 2021’s ‘Top Companies for Women to Work For in Transportation’ unveiled
feed.summary: The Women In Trucking Association (WIT) has released its annual list of "Top Companies for Women to Work For in Transportation" for 2021. These companies were recognized for fostering positive environments for women, offering gender-diverse cultures, competitive compensation, flexible work options, and professional development opportunities. The list celebrates efforts to attract and retain a more diverse workforce in the transportation industry.

feed.title: MakeMyTrip partners Amazon Pay to offer travel services
feed.summary: Amazon Pay and MakeMyTrip India have announced a strategic partnership to offer travel services, including flights, hotels, and bus bookings, on Amazon.in. This collaboration aims to provide Amazon customers with easy access to MakeMyTrip's offerings and expand MakeMyTrip's reach, particularly in smaller cities and towns. The partnership leverages Amazon Pay's extensive customer base and payment solutions, forecasting growth in India's online travel services market.

feed.title: Pinterest Announces ‘Pinterest TV’ Shoppable Live-Stream Series
feed.summary: Pinterest is launching "Pinterest TV," a new series of shoppable live-stream events beginning November 8th, to capitalize on the growing live commerce trend. These daily episodes will feature creators, exclusive deals from brands, and interactive chat functions. The platform is also creating a virtual studio to help more creators produce their own live shopping streams within the app, further boosting its e-commerce focus.

feed.title: Amazon, Sleep Number lease industrial spaces in Greater Cincinnati
feed.summary: Amazon.com and Sleep Number have leased industrial spaces in Greater Cincinnati's Springdale Commerce Park. This development adds two significant new tenants to the industrial park, signaling continued growth in the region's commercial real estate sector.

feed.title: Will same-day deliver holiday cheer for Abercrombie & Fitch?
feed.summary: Abercrombie & Fitch Co. has launched same-day delivery across its entire U.S. store fleet, including its Abercrombie & Fitch, Abercrombie Kids, Hollister, and Gilly Hicks banners, through partnerships with Uber, Shipt, Postmates, Roadie, and Delivery Solutions. This initiative aims to diversify its carrier base and enhance its final mile delivery capacity, especially as it positions more inventory in stores to fulfill e-commerce orders amid supply chain constraints. The new service costs $10, making it more affordable than next-day and two-day delivery options and complementing existing omnichannel solutions

feed.title: Smart thermostat pioneer ecobee, facing growing threats from Amazon, sells to U.S. generator maker for up to $770-million
feed.summary: Ecobee Inc., a Toronto-based smart thermostat pioneer, has been sold to U.S. generator maker Generac Holdings Inc. for up to US$770-million. This acquisition comes as Amazon, previously an investor and partner, is becoming a direct competitor with its own smart thermostat. Generac aims to integrate ecobee into its strategy of turning buildings into "microgrids" focused on energy management and conservation.

feed.title: Ecobee acquired by generator company Generac for $770 million
feed.summary: Generac Holdings has acquired Toronto-based smart home thermostat company Ecobee for $770 million, with the deal expected to close in the fourth quarter. Ecobee's CEO and founder, Stuart Lombard, will continue in his role, and the company will operate as a subsidiary of Generac, expanding its Toronto office. This acquisition combines Ecobee's smart home technology with Generac's energy management solutions, aiming to create a comprehensive home energy ecosystem.

feed.title: MakeMyTrip and Amazon Pay form strategic tie-up to offer travel services
feed.summary: MakeMyTrip India and Amazon Pay have announced a strategic long-term partnership to offer travel services on Amazon.in, including its mobile apps and websites. This alliance will allow MakeMyTrip to extend its distribution to Amazon Pay's large user base, particularly in smaller towns, and accelerate online booking for travel services. For Amazon Pay, the partnership aims to provide a convenient experience and greater value to its customers by offering access to MakeMyTrip's travel services.

feed.title: Facebook to shut down facial recognition system, delete face prints of 1 billion users
feed.summary: Facebook has announced it will shut down its facial recognition system and delete the face prints of over a billion users due to growing concerns about the technology's misuse and increasing regulatory scrutiny. This move, described as one of the largest shifts in facial recognition use, comes amidst a crisis for Facebook (now Meta) and follows years of criticism regarding privacy and the ethics of such technology. While the company stated this specific broad identification method would cease, it did not rule out using facial recognition for narrower authentication purposes in future products, particularly for on-device applications.

feed.title: Tesla Surges to Lift Markets Into New Record Close
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. has surpassed a $1 trillion market value, becoming the most valuable car company, driven by a large order from Hertz and its plan to open its supercharging network to other EV brands. This surge has positively impacted the broader market, with the S&P 500 reaching new highs, and boosting the consumer discretionary sector. The American Century Focused Dynamic Growth ETF (FDG) holds Tesla among its top investments, benefiting from the company's strong performance.

feed.title: Quiet Logistics acquired for $350 million by its client, American Eagle Outfitters
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters has acquired Quiet Logistics, a third-party logistics provider, for $350 million. Quiet Logistics will operate as a wholly-owned subsidiary, continuing to serve AEO while also expanding its customer base. This acquisition is part of AEO's strategy to transform its supply chain for greater agility and speed, following its earlier purchase of Airterra Inc.

feed.title: American Eagle Outfitters Acquires Additional Logistics Firm, Quiet Logistics, for $350 Million
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters has acquired the logistics firm Quiet Logistics for $350 million in cash. This acquisition builds on their earlier purchase of AirTerra, aiming to enhance their supply chain capabilities, improve delivery speed, and create an on-demand operations platform. The retailer plans to leverage Quiet Logistics' services for its brands and expand them to other businesses, expecting significant benefits in speed and efficiency.

feed.title: Nature’s Bounty unveils jelly bean vitamin line
feed.summary: Nature's Bounty has launched a new line of fruit-flavored, vegetarian jelly bean vitamins for both adults and children. These vitamins are designed to support various wellness needs, including immune health, sleep, and overall multi-vitamin support, without artificial sweeteners or gelatin. The company aims to make wellness routines more enjoyable and accessible, with products available at major retailers and online.

feed.title: Plug Power establishing green hydrogen facility in Young County
feed.summary: Plug Power is developing a green hydrogen production facility in Young County, Texas, which will be one of 13 plants aimed at producing 500 tons of hydrogen daily. The facility will be powered by the Apex Clean Energy Young Wind farm and will utilize treated wastewater from the city of Graham, offering a beneficial water reuse solution for the community. This project is expected to create 50 high-paying local jobs and leverages the area's existing knowledge base from oil and gas development.

feed.title: Amazon wants to open a new office building in Jersey City
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly close to a deal for a significant office space expansion in Jersey City, New Jersey, seeking roughly 400,000 square feet in the Harborside 1 building. This move indicates Amazon's continued growth within the New York City region, despite the earlier collapse of its second headquarters plan in Queens. The e-commerce giant has also been expanding its presence in Manhattan with recent lease agreements and property purchases.

feed.title: JBG Smith to buy Union Market property as company swings back to profit
feed.summary: JBG Smith announced its intention to acquire a significant property near Union Market, coinciding with the company reporting quarterly net income for the first time in over a year. The acquired property, "The Batley," is an 11-story apartment building with retail space. This move signals a positive financial turnaround for JBG Smith.

feed.title: Amazon, Salesforce, Among 45 Companies Awarded Terra Carta Seal From Prince Charles
feed.summary: Prince Charles' Sustainable Markets Initiative awarded the Terra Carta Seal to 45 companies, including Amazon and Salesforce, for their commitment to sustainability and credible transition roadmaps toward net-zero emissions. The award, designed by Sir Jony Ive, recognizes companies leading their industries in working towards the goals of the Paris Agreement. Corporate Knights partnered with the initiative to review applications, ensuring adherence to rigorous sustainability metrics.

feed.title: Lovett Industrial completes first 3 Houston-area warehouses, including 2 for Amazon
feed.summary: Lovett Industrial has completed its first three warehouses in the Houston area, two of which are occupied by Amazon, including the 99 North Logistics Park in Spring. This development highlights the growth of the industrial real estate market in Houston, with a new company co-owned by Frank Liu capitalizing on this trend.

feed.title: To Win Customer Loyalty, Make Customers Feel Valued, Appreciated, And Respected
feed.summary: Forrester's annual Customer Experience Index (CX Index™) reveals that emotional connections significantly impact customer loyalty more than effectiveness or ease. The study found that making customers feel valued, appreciated, and respected are the most influential positive emotions, while frustration, annoyance, and disappointment are the most dominant negative emotions. Companies should prioritize setting and meeting reasonable expectations to foster loyalty and avoid negative customer experiences.

feed.title: Amazon Workspaces review
feed.summary: This review of Amazon WorkSpaces, Amazon's Desktop as a Service (DaaS) offering, highlights its affordability and ease of deployment for virtual systems. While it supports both Windows and Linux, the article notes a lack of advanced management tools for larger deployments and some limitations regarding secure file transfer and older OS versions. The service is presented as a flexible solution for businesses, especially those with dynamic needs or remote workforces, despite some performance inconsistencies and minor inconveniences in user management.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty partners with DoorDash on same-day delivery
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty has partnered with DoorDash to offer same-day delivery of over 25,000 personal care products. This service is initially available in six major U.S. markets, with plans for expansion in 2022. The collaboration utilizes DoorDash Drive, DoorDash's white-label fulfillment platform, to meet increasing consumer demand for rapid delivery services, especially during the holiday season.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc. leases new shipping facility short walk from monster Mt. Juliet hub
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has leased a new 610,000-square-foot shipping facility at 23 Athlete's Way in Mt. Juliet, Tennessee. This new facility is located near Amazon's existing large shipping hub that opened recently. The building was constructed speculatively by Panattoni Development Co.

feed.title: Strategy Analytics: Ring, Nest, and Arlo Remain Among the Leaders in the Smart Home Camera Market
feed.summary: Strategy Analytics reports that Ring, Nest, and Arlo continue to lead the smart home camera market, but a shift towards software-centric business models is underway. The report suggests that hardware-first models are becoming unsustainable, with companies like Amazon and Google, who are already investing in software, set to further dominate the market. Future success in this sector will depend on advanced software and services rather than just hardware specifications.

feed.title: Amazon Launches FamilyFlex to Give Front-line Employees Flexibility and Support and to Help Parents Transition Back to the Workplace
feed.summary: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) has launched FamilyFlex, a new program designed to provide front-line employees with greater flexibility and comprehensive benefits, particularly to help parents re-enter the workforce. The program includes options like shift swapping, custom schedules, Anytime Shifts, and Anytime Pay, alongside extensive benefits such as parental leave, childcare resources, mental health support, and adoption assistance. This initiative aims to address the challenges faced by working families and support employees in balancing their work and personal lives.

feed.title: Monthly Market Wrap - October 2021
feed.summary: The October 2021 market wrap highlights a month of economic observations including rising inflation, the Federal Reserve's anticipated tapering, and varied performance across domestic and international equities. It also covers positive trends in alternative investments and the increasing integration of ESG factors into investment strategies. The report details specific market indices and economic indicators, providing a comprehensive overview of the financial landscape during the month.

feed.title: Illumina Supports Canada's Nationwide COVID-19 Genome Sequencing Initiative
feed.summary: Illumina announced its world-class sequencing and bioinformatics solutions are being used by HostSeq, part of the Canadian COVID-19 Genomics Network (CanCOGeN), to identify biomarkers and support novel therapeutics for COVID-19. The initiative involves sequencing the genomes of up to 10,000 patients in Canada, utilizing Illumina Connected Analytics (ICA) for secure data sharing among researchers. Illumina also supports VirusSeq, another national initiative sequencing SARS-CoV-2 viral genomes to track transmission trends and identify evolving viral characteristics.

feed.title: Lovesac partners with Amazon Prime Video in launch of Sactionals StealthTech Sound + Charge
feed.summary: The Lovesac Company has launched its Sactionals StealthTech Sound + Charge, an innovative home furnishing that seamlessly integrates immersive surround sound by Harman Kardon and wireless charging into its adaptable Sactionals platform. This system aims to provide a superior audio experience without the clutter of visible speakers and wires, adapting to various configurations and fabric choices. Lovesac founder and CEO Shawn Nelson highlights the system's ability to offer a personalized and high-quality surround sound experience for different lifestyles.

feed.title: QVC was ahead of its time
feed.summary: The article argues that QVC was ahead of its time, comparing its transparent selling approach to the "shoppable" videos on platforms like Instagram and TikTok today. It suggests that while social media often blurs the lines between content and commerce, the direct sales pitches of QVC and its modern counterparts offer a refreshing transparency, creating a sense of agency for shoppers. The author highlights the concept of "parasocial relationships" fostered by QVC hosts and influencers as a key to their selling success.

feed.title: Kroger is taking on Publix in Florida without opening a single grocery store — and building a blueprint for its future
feed.summary: Kroger is entering the Florida market using a robot-powered warehouse and delivery service, rather than traditional grocery stores, to compete with entrenched rivals like Publix. This strategy aims to make e-commerce a more profitable venture for the country's largest supermarket operator and serve as a blueprint for expansion into other new markets like the Northeast. Despite the high capital investment, Kroger aims to leverage this model to gain market share by offering a distinct delivery-focused service.

feed.title: Trii ofrece gratis 1.500 transacciones del fondo bursátil Hcolsel de Global X en la bolsa
feed.summary: Trii is offering 1,500 free transactions for the Hcolsel ETF from Global X on the Colombian Stock Exchange, along with cashbacks and referral codes. This initiative follows the recent authorization for Trii to allow trading of international stocks in Colombia, including major US and Chilean companies. The Hcolsel ETF itself is composed of significant Colombian companies like Ecopetrol and Bancolombia.

feed.title: Giving Lee and Wrangler Their Voices Back
feed.summary: Following their 2019 spinoff from VF Corp. into Kontoor Brands Inc., Lee and Wrangler have demonstrated unexpected growth and revitalization, overcoming the pandemic. CEO Scott Baxter explains that the separation allowed for significant investment in marketing and global campaigns, giving the "iconic American denim" brands a new voice and reaching a broader audience. Kontoor Brands has reported strong financial results and revised its outlook upward, emphasizing a strategy of "winning with the winners" through key wholesale accounts.

feed.title: Focus: Amazon takes another swipe at union as Alabama rematch looms
feed.summary: Amazon is intensifying its anti-union campaign in Bessemer, Alabama, ahead of a potential second union vote after its initial victory was challenged. The company is using mandatory meetings, bathroom stall posters, and flying in staff to discourage unionization, while the RWDSU union is stepping up its organizing efforts, including home visits. Both sides are employing aggressive tactics as the stakes for union representation at Amazon's U.S. facilities remain high.

feed.title: American Eagle Outfitters is literally serious about owning its supply chain
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) is expanding its control over its supply chain by acquiring Quiet Logistics for $350 million, marking its second such acquisition in recent months. This strategic move aims to improve delivery speed, reliability, and cost-effectiveness, offering competitive advantages amidst widespread supply chain disruptions. The acquisition, building on previous investments like AirTerra, is expected to enhance AEO's in-market fulfillment network and operational agility.

feed.title: Bed Bath & Beyond Introduces Digital Marketplace
feed.summary: Bed Bath & Beyond has launched a new digital marketplace allowing third-party sellers to list curated baby and home products, integrating it into their existing digital platform. This move positions the retailer alongside Amazon, Walmart, and Target in offering such marketplaces. Additionally, Bed Bath & Beyond recently announced a collaboration with Kroger, expanding its reach for baby and home items through Kroger's online and select physical stores.

feed.title: Google acquires $1B stake in CME Group as companies ink 10-year cloud deal
feed.summary: CME Group Inc., a major operator of financial exchanges, has partnered with Google LLC for a 10-year cloud deal to migrate its technology infrastructure to Google Cloud. As part of this agreement, Google made a significant investment, acquiring $1 billion worth of CME Group stock. This partnership aims to scale CME Group's IT infrastructure, streamline operations, and enhance various technology capabilities, including risk mitigation tools and data analytics services for clients.

feed.title: Prime Gaming and Electronic Arts Collaborate to Bring Players Free Games and In-Game Content for EA’s Biggest Titles, Starting with Apex Legends
feed.summary: Amazon's Prime Gaming and Electronic Arts are partnering to offer Amazon Prime members free games and in-game content for popular EA titles through 2022. This collaboration includes exclusive content for Apex Legends and Battlefield 2042, along with free PC games like Dragon Age: Inquisition and Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit Remastered. The initiative aims to provide significant value to over 200 million Amazon Prime members worldwide.

feed.title: Amazon buys Robinson Ranch land, reveals timeline for new warehouse
feed.summary: Amazon has purchased the Robinson Ranch land in Round Rock for a new warehouse and revealed its timeline. The e-commerce giant closed on the land in late October after previously submitting an application to the city to rezone and annex the site. This move contributes to Amazon's growing presence in the Austin metro, where it is nearing 12,000 employees.

feed.title: BJ's Wholesale chain plans Middle Tennessee expansion
feed.summary: BJ's Wholesale Club, based in Westborough, MA, has announced its plans for expansion into Tennessee, specifically targeting Middle Tennessee. The retail chain intends to open a new location in La Vergne, marking its entry into the Nashville business journal's coverage area. This expansion signifies the company's growth strategy into new regional markets.

feed.title: Kroger launches Boost membership program
feed.summary: Kroger has introduced "Boost," a new membership program offering two tiers of no-fee delivery services and double fuel points to expand its online grocery business. This program aims to compete with similar offerings from Walmart and Amazon, providing a tiered approach to delivery speed and a welcome kit worth over $100 to offset membership costs. Boost is rolling out in several divisions with plans for further expansion, building on Kroger's growing e-commerce and vertically integrated delivery network.

feed.title: Amazon opening 3 new retail stores, including one in Columbia
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is opening three new brick-and-mortar retail stores in the Maryland and Washington D.C. region. This includes two Amazon 4-star locations, one at The Mall in Columbia and another in Germantown, and an Amazon Pop Up store in Washington, D.C. The 4-star stores will sell highly-rated items from Amazon's website, while the Pop Up location will offer themed selections, likely tied to the holiday season.

feed.title: JD.com: Not Just A Traditional E-commerce Platform
feed.summary: JD.com, China's second-largest e-commerce platform, recently reported strong Q2 2021 results with record revenue and a significant increase in active customers. The company is structured into three main segments: Retail, Logistics, and New Business, highlighting its diversification beyond traditional e-commerce. Despite regulatory challenges in China, JD.com's tangible presence in the economy and direct employment of a large workforce may position it favorably for long-term growth.

feed.title: Rivian Fleet: Electric Work & Commercial Vans
feed.summary: Rivian is electrifying commercial fleets with its new Rivian Commercial Van (RCV), designed for sustainability, cost-effectiveness, and driver safety. The RCV offers significant reductions in operational GHG emissions compared to traditional internal combustion vehicles. Rivian also provides support for fleet purchases, servicing, and integration of their R1T and R1S models into commercial operations.

feed.title: Google invests $1bn into Chicago Mercantile Exchange as part of cloud migration deal
feed.summary: Google Cloud has signed a 10-year deal with CME Group to migrate all of CME's infrastructure and markets to the cloud starting in early 2022. As part of this agreement, Google has also made a $1 billion equity investment in CME Group. The partnership aims to enhance CME's services, introduce new products, and accelerate technological innovation in capital markets while strengthening Google Cloud's position in the financial services sector.

feed.title: ‘Business needs a noble purpose and a human-centric approach — these core principles revived Best Buy’
feed.summary: Hubert Joly, former CEO of Best Buy, discusses how a human-centric approach and noble purpose revived the company, leading its share price to rise 11-fold. He emphasizes that putting people at the center and embracing all stakeholders is crucial for businesses today, especially in navigating challenges like the pandemic and climate change. Joly argues that the "Milton Friedman doctrine" of profit-first is outdated and highlights companies like Microsoft, Ralph Lauren, and Unilever as examples of successful human-centric models.

feed.title: Jeff Bezos Raises $3.3 Billion by Selling Amazon Stock This Week
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos sold $1.3 billion worth of Amazon stock on Friday, bringing his total sales for the week to $3.3 billion. These sales contribute to the $9.9 billion in Amazon stock he has sold throughout the current year, often to fund ventures like Blue Origin and the Earth Fund.

feed.title: How Intel plans to catch Samsung and TSMC and regain its dominance in the chip market
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has outlined an ambitious plan to regain dominance in the chip market by 2025, aiming to surpass competitors Samsung and TSMC. The strategy involves significant investment in new fabrication plants globally, totaling over $44 billion, and opening Intel's fabs to other companies through Intel Foundry Services. This move addresses past production missteps and aims to leverage government subsidies like the CHIPS Act to boost U.S. chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Migrating from Dell EMC PowerScale to Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
feed.summary: This article details the process of migrating data from Dell EMC PowerScale (Isilon or OneFS) to Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP, a fully managed native AWS storage service. It highlights the use of NetApp Cloud Sync for a straightforward data migration, outlining the necessary networking prerequisites and step-by-step instructions for setting up the migration within NetApp Cloud Manager. The article concludes by emphasizing the benefits of FSx for NetApp ONTAP as a highly available, high-performance, and feature-rich storage solution for enterprise workloads in the AWS cloud.

feed.title: EXPLAINED: What is Metaverse and Why Facebook/ Meta Thinks it's the Future of Internet
feed.summary: This article explains the concept of the metaverse, why Facebook (now Meta) views it as the future of the internet, and what its potential applications are. It details how the metaverse would work, from virtual interactions and business opportunities to the technologies being developed to build it, while also touching on data privacy concerns.

feed.title: AMD shares close up 10% after company wins Meta partnership, announces new chips
feed.summary: AMD shares surged 10% after the company announced a partnership with Meta (formerly Facebook) as a chip customer and unveiled a new range of chips at its Accelerated Data Center Premiere event. This move strengthens AMD's position against competitors like Intel, as the company continues to gain market share with its data center chip sales experiencing significant growth. Additional details about the Meta partnership are expected to be revealed later in the week at the Open Compute Project summit.

feed.title: Poll Finds Gamers Are Most Interested in EA, Rockstar of Major Publishers
feed.summary: A Morning Consult poll found that Electronic Arts (EA) and Rockstar Games are the top publishers capturing gamers' interest, with 64% and 61% of avid gamers interested, respectively. Despite controversies, Ubisoft and Activision also ranked highly. CD Projekt Red, however, ranked near the bottom, likely due to issues with its Cyberpunk 2077 launch.

feed.title: Resideo Adds Amazon Smart Thermostat to Utility Programs
feed.summary: Resideo announced that the Amazon Smart Thermostat will be integrated into its Connected Savings® portfolio, allowing utility customers to participate in rewards programs. Built with Honeywell Home Thermostat Technology, this integration aims to enhance grid reliability and promote energy savings. Resideo and Amazon are partnering to drive enrollments in energy efficiency programs, supporting green energy initiatives and helping utilities achieve net-zero goals.

feed.title: TreeHouse Foods explores sale option after pressure from investor
feed.summary: TreeHouse Foods Inc. is considering a sale of the company or a divestment of a unit after pressure from hedge fund Jana Partners, which argued the packaged food maker was undervalued. Jana Partners, known for pushing changes in the food and beverage industry, holds about 9% of TreeHouse and has appointed two independent directors to its board. This development comes as TreeHouse reported better-than-expected third-quarter revenue but forecast full-year adjusted profit below estimates.

feed.title: Apollo Hires Former Amazon Executive to Bolster ESG Firepower
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management has appointed Carletta Ooton, a former Amazon.com executive, as its head of ESG for private equity and an operating partner in its portfolio performance solutions group. This move aims to strengthen Apollo's environmental, social, and governance expertise, reflecting a broader trend of high-profile ESG hires by the firm. Ms. Ooton will assist Apollo's portfolio companies in integrating ESG principles into their operations.

feed.title: Macy's to lift minimum wage to $15 per hour as labor market tightens
feed.summary: Macy's Inc. announced it will raise its minimum wage for over 100,000 U.S. employees to $15 per hour by May, responding to a tightening labor market and competitive hiring landscape. The department store chain will also invest $35 million in a debt-free education benefit program for its workers over the next four years. This move follows similar actions by other major retailers like Amazon and Walmart to attract and retain staff amidst a nationwide labor shortage.

feed.title: Blue Light Special to bust: Michigan's last Kmart about to end era
feed.summary: Michigan's last Kmart store, located in Marshall, is scheduled to close on November 21, 2021, marking the end of a nearly 60-year history for the once-innovative discount retailer in its home state. The closure highlights Kmart's decline due to a lack of investment and inability to compete with rivals like Walmart and Target, despite its deep roots in Michigan and its pioneering role in big-box retail. The article explores Kmart's rise and fall, its impact on local communities like Marshall, and the lingering legacy for former employees.

feed.title: Monmouth REIT shareholder revolt leads to $4B sale, $600M better than before
feed.summary: Monmouth Real Estate Investment Corp. has agreed to be acquired by Industrial Logistics Properties Trust for $4 billion after shareholders rejected an earlier, lower bid from Sam Zell's Equity Commonwealth. The all-cash deal, valued at $21 per share, is approximately $600 million higher than the previous offer and has been welcomed by activist investor Blackwells Capital. This acquisition will significantly expand Industrial Logistics Property Trust's mainland portfolio, building on Monmouth REIT's success in industrial properties.

feed.title: Publix is getting some new competition. And this grocer isn’t even opening a supermarket
feed.summary: Kroger is expanding its online grocery delivery service into Florida, including Central and soon South Florida, without opening traditional brick-and-mortar stores. This move introduces a new form of competition for established Florida grocers like Publix, Winn-Dixie, and Fresco y Más, focusing on a fulfillment center model with robots for delivery. The article details Kroger's strategy, the growth of online grocery shopping fueled by the pandemic, and how local competitors are responding to this new market entry.

feed.title: On the heels of going public, DocGo aims to expand mobile medical services in the U.S. and overseas
feed.summary: DocGo, formerly Ambulnz, went public via a SPAC merger with Motion Acquisition Corp., valuing the company at $1.1 billion. The company plans to use the $158 million raised to expand its mobile health services geographically and through M&A opportunities, both domestically and internationally, including a direct-to-consumer pilot program. DocGo differentiates itself by using licensed/certified EMTs, paramedics, and LPNs to provide cost-effective "last-mile" healthcare services.

feed.title: Amazon considers Georgetown site for large warehouse
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly considering a site in Georgetown for a large "package sortation center" and "warehouse for overnight processing of packages for long-haul delivery." This development would further solidify Amazon's presence as one of the largest employers in the Austin area, where it already had 11,000 direct employees as of August. The project details were disclosed in a state filing, indicating significant expansion plans for the online retail giant.

feed.title: eBay Launches ‘Refurbished’ Offering
feed.summary: eBay has launched "eBay Refurbished," a new online destination for purchasing like-new products from popular brands like Apple, Samsung, and Dyson, all backed by a one or two-year warranty. The initiative aims to address holiday scarcity issues and changing consumer behaviors, offering discounted inventory sourced from premium brands or top-rated sellers. The program expands on eBay's previous Certified Refurbished offering, including multiple condition grades and leading Allstate warranties, free shipping, and a money-back guarantee, catering to a growing trend of consumers buying and gifting refurbished items for sustainability and value.

feed.title: Elon Musk’s Net Worth Has Plunged $50 Billion in 2 Days
feed.summary: Elon Musk's net worth has dropped by $50 billion in two days following a significant plunge in Tesla shares. This decline, the largest two-day fall on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index, was influenced by Musk's Twitter poll about selling his stock, his brother's pre-poll stock sale, and a report suggesting Musk might need to sell shares to cover personal debts. Despite the substantial loss, Musk's fortune is still up 70% for the year, and Tesla's market capitalization remains over $1 trillion.

feed.title: BD Tops COVID Testing Market With Veritor Rollout, BARDA Contract
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson's smartphone-based rapid antigen test for COVID-19, the Veritor kit, is now commercially available and has seen a significant price drop due to a partnership between the Biden administration and major retailers. The company also secured a $24.7 million contract from BARDA, potentially rising to $40.3 million, to develop combination diagnostic tests for various infectious diseases. This initiative aims to shore up COVID-19 diagnostics for the long term and streamline testing processes.

feed.title: Google-owner Alphabet breaches $2 trillion market cap, joins Apple and Microsoft
feed.summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., reached a $2 trillion market capitalization for the first time, driven by increased digital ad spending and growth in its cloud business. This achievement places Alphabet alongside Apple and Microsoft in the exclusive $2 trillion club. The company reported a record third quarter in 2021 with $65.1 billion in earnings.

feed.title: Amazon And Other Big Winners In Rivian’s Supercharged IPO
feed.summary: Rivian's anticipated IPO is set to be the largest of 2021, showcasing significant investor confidence in the electric vehicle market despite Rivian being largely pre-revenue. Major stakeholders like Amazon, T. Rowe Price, Global Oryx Company Ltd., Ford Motor Co., and Manheim Investments are poised for substantial financial gains from the public offering. Their investments, primarily made in the past few years, reflect a strong bet on the future of electric transportation.

feed.title: Check out Hertz Global Holdings Equity Warrant Exp 30th June 2051's stock price (HTZWW) in real time
feed.summary: This CNBC article provides real-time stock price information for Hertz Global Holdings Equity Warrant Exp 30th June 2051 (HTZWW). It includes current trading data, key statistics, financial ratios, and recent news related to Hertz. The article also provides a company profile outlining Hertz's operations and leadership.

feed.title: Google loses challenge against EU antitrust ruling, $2.8-bln fine
authors: Francois Aulner and Foo Yun Chee
feed.summary: Alphabet's Google unit lost an appeal against a 2.42-billion-euro ($2.8-billion) antitrust decision by the EU related to its price comparison shopping service. This ruling marks a significant win for Europe's competition chief, Margrethe Vestager, and could strengthen the EU's position in ongoing investigations into other major tech companies like Amazon, Apple, and Facebook. Google stated it would review the judgment and has already complied with the Commission's order to ensure a level playing field.

feed.title: Affirm shares bounce on new Amazon deal and strong guidance
feed.summary: Affirm Holdings Inc. saw its shares rebound significantly after announcing an expanded partnership with Amazon.com Inc. and providing optimistic revenue guidance for the December quarter. The new deal makes Affirm Amazon's exclusive third-party, non-credit card, buy now, pay later option in the U.S. and is expected to drive strong financial performance, with the company predicting higher-than-expected revenue for fiscal 2022.

feed.title: 5 Charts on General Electric's Fall From Grace
feed.summary: This article details General Electric's significant decline in value over the past two decades, from once being the most valuable company globally to its current 75th position in the U.S. stock market. It attributes GE's fall to a deterioration of culture, poor capital allocation decisions, and an over-reliance on its financial unit. The article discusses GE's recent decision to split into three companies (aviation, healthcare, and energy), a move applauded by analysts and seen as a potential catalyst for closing the persistent price/value gap for the stock.

feed.title: 3M inks 410K sf lease renewal for DeKalb distribution center
feed.summary: Industrial giant 3M signed a 12-year lease renewal for a 410,000-square-foot distribution center in DeKalb, Illinois, making it one of the largest industrial lease transactions in the state for Q4. The renewal, with landlord Modiv, will commence in August 2022 and highlights the growing demand for warehouse space driven by e-commerce expansion. The facility is crucial for storing and exporting 3M products globally, with two additional 3M warehouses located nearby.

feed.title: The Children’s Place launches tween brand Sugar & Jade
feed.summary: The Children's Place launched its new tween brand, Sugar & Jade, in response to the growing $8 billion tween apparel market. The direct-to-consumer brand offers sizes 8 to 22 with free shipping and returns, targeting existing Children's Place customers transitioning to their next style stage. This move is part of broader industry changes with other retailers also expanding into the tween category.

feed.title: 26-Year-Old Alex Rodrigues Becomes Youngest Current CEO Of A Publicly Traded Company With $5 Billion Embark SPAC Merger
feed.summary: Alex Rodrigues, at 26, has become the youngest current CEO of a publicly traded company after his autonomous trucking software company, Embark, went public via a $5 billion SPAC merger. Rodrigues, who developed a self-driving golf cart at 19, founded Embark to focus on 18-wheelers, aiming to revolutionize the trucking industry. He is estimated to be worth $500 million and lives a minimalist lifestyle, focused on growing Embark, which aims to fulfill over 14,200 reservations for Embark-enabled trucks by 2024.

feed.title: With sky-high consumer demand, Black Friday sales could set records this year
feed.summary: Record consumer demand due to coronavirus relief spending and a strong economy is expected to lead to record-setting Black Friday and holiday sales, despite ongoing supply chain bottlenecks. While online shopping has driven much of this surge, experts anticipate a return to in-person Black Friday crowds due to nostalgia after last year's lockdowns. However, shoppers should be prepared for potential item availability issues and labor shortages in stores.

feed.title: Facebook Is Under Fire, but It's Also Undervalued
feed.summary: Despite ongoing controversies, regulatory scrutiny, and a company name change to Meta Platforms, Morningstar's analysis suggests that the stock is undervalued, trading at a 16% discount to its fair value estimate. The article highlights Meta's two business segments—its legacy apps and the new Reality Labs for metaverse development—and discusses the potential impacts of regulatory and antitrust risks, concluding that the stock remains the most attractive social-media play in Morningstar's coverage. The company's revenue growth is primarily driven by advertising, leveraging its vast user base and the shift towards online spending, while its wide economic moat is based on network effects and intangible data assets.

feed.title: Can Build-A-Bear replicate the experience online?
feed.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop is launching an interactive online platform called "Bear Builder 3D Workshop" to replicate its in-store customization experience. This move comes as the company observed over 20% online market growth during the pandemic, aligning with a broader trend of consumers increasingly shopping for toys and back-to-school items online. Build-A-Bear aims to meet consumers where they are, betting on the continued shift to digital retail.

feed.title: IBM, Amazon partner to extend reach of data tools for oil companies
feed.summary: IBM and Amazon Web Services are collaborating to expand the accessibility of data management tools for oil companies. This partnership addresses the issue of data residency requirements in some oil-producing countries by enabling companies to use IBM's OpenShift technology to store data in privately owned data centers. The initiative also aims to help oil companies integrate renewable energy assets, such as wind and solar, into their portfolios by providing a unified platform for diverse energy data.

feed.title: HyperX Cloud Flight Wireless Gaming Headset Lineup Expands to Include PS5 Support
feed.summary: HyperX announced the expansion of its Cloud Flight wireless gaming headset lineup to include compatibility with PlayStation 5, alongside PlayStation 4. The updated headset features a 2.4GHz wireless connection, up to 30 hours of battery life, and a new mic mute LED. It also boasts HyperX's signature comfort with durable steel sliders and memory foam, 50mm drivers for immersive audio, and a detachable noise-cancelling microphone, retailing for $119.99 on Amazon.

feed.title: Target releases Black Friday ad with deals on electronics, toys and more starting Sunday
feed.summary: Target has released its Black Friday ad, announcing a weeklong sale starting Sunday, November 21st, and running through November 27th, with additional deals launching on Thanksgiving Day. Stores will be closed on Thanksgiving but reopen Black Friday morning. This year, Target's holiday price match guarantee allows shoppers to request price adjustments on items purchased within the window if the price drops later in the season.

feed.title: American Tower nears $10 billion deal to buy CoreSite
feed.summary: American Tower Corp. is set to acquire CoreSite Realty Corp. in a deal valued at approximately $10.1 billion, including debt. The acquisition reflects ongoing consolidation in the data center REIT sector, driven by increasing demand for data infrastructure and cloud computing services. CoreSite operates 24 data centers and generates revenue not only from leasing but also from power and interconnection services.

feed.title: IonQ Announces Third Quarter 2021 Financial Results
feed.summary: IonQ announced its third-quarter 2021 financial results, highlighting total contract value (TCV) bookings of $15.1 million year-to-date and updated its 2021 TCV bookings guidance to $15.8 million at the midpoint. The company also reported significant technological milestones, including industry-first error correction demonstration and superior quantum computer performance, alongside strategic collaborations and a strong financial position after its business combination. Financial highlights included $223 thousand in revenue for the quarter, $587 million in cash and cash equivalents, and a net loss of $14.8 million.

feed.title: Verizon introduces hands-free calling via Alexa at Home
feed.summary: Verizon has launched Number Share - Home, allowing customers to make and receive hands-free audio calls on Alexa-enabled devices using their mobile number. This new feature aims to provide convenience and safety, enabling calls even when a phone is out of reach, and includes support for emergency services and caller ID. New subscribers who purchase an Alexa device from Verizon can receive up to three months free of the service.

feed.title: BP Expanding RNG Venture with Clean Energy Fuels into Iowa, South Dakota
feed.summary: BP plc is expanding its renewable natural gas (RNG) venture with Clean Energy Fuels Corp. to develop new projects on dairy farms in Iowa and South Dakota. These farms, housing over 30,000 cows, have the potential to produce more than 7 million gallons/year of RNG from methane, with the first production facility expected to be operational next year. This expansion aligns with BP's goal for net-zero carbon emissions and addresses the rapidly growing demand for RNG.

feed.title: Netflix releases weekly list of top shows by viewing hours
feed.summary: Netflix has begun releasing weekly reports of its top shows and movies by viewing hours globally, a move towards greater transparency in streaming viewership data. This new system breaks out top 10 lists for English and non-English titles, with "Squid Game" dominating non-English TV and "Red Notice" leading English-language films recently. The initiative addresses past criticisms of streamers for selectively sharing data and aims to provide a clearer understanding of success in the streaming world.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel opening in east El Paso next year
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel is expanding its presence in El Paso with a new location planned for the Eastlake Marketplace, near the Amazon fulfillment center. This will be the second Cracker Barrel in El Paso and is expected to open in Spring 2022, creating over 100 full and part-time jobs.

feed.title: Amazon Nixes Jersey City Office Deal in Setback for Landlord
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has cancelled a planned office expansion in Jersey City, which would have seen the company lease approximately 400,000 square feet of space from Mack-Cali Realty Corp. This decision represents a setback for the landlord, as Amazon had been nearing a deal for the waterfront property. Amazon has been expanding its presence in the New York City area following its withdrawal from the HQ2 project.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel opening in east El Paso next year
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel is expanding its presence in El Paso with a new Old Country Store breaking ground at the Eastlake Marketplace on the eastside, near an Amazon fulfillment center. The new location plans to hire over 100 full and part-time employees and is expected to open in Spring 2022. This will be the second Cracker Barrel in El Paso, adding another option for residents to enjoy its traditional American comfort food.

feed.title: Walmart says it's ready for holidays — but hasn't yet seen huge wave of early shoppers
feed.summary: Walmart CFO Brett Biggs stated that while the company is prepared for the holiday season with increased inventory, it has not yet observed a significant surge of early holiday shoppers, unlike competitors Amazon and Target who began promotions in October. Walmart strategically delayed its major holiday sales until November, focusing on strong grocery sales in Q3. Analysts believe Walmart's robust inventory and large store footprint position it well to navigate supply chain issues and meet customer demand during the holiday shopping period.

feed.title: Amazon adds 2 last-mile delivery centers in Greater Cincinnati
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is expanding its logistics network in Greater Cincinnati by adding two last-mile distribution centers. One center has already opened in West Chester, and another is slated to open in Florence in 2022. These new facilities will enhance Amazon's delivery capabilities in the region.

feed.title: Instacart Business Model
feed.summary: This article details the Instacart business model, covering its history, ownership, mission, revenue streams (commissions, delivery fees, subscriptions, advertising), and a comprehensive Business Model Canvas analysis. It also examines Instacart's customer segments, value propositions, channels, key resources, activities, partners, cost structure, and a SWOT analysis, concluding on its market dominance despite growing competition.

feed.title: Walmart stock tumbles as supply chain snarls hit margins ahead of holidays
feed.summary: Walmart's shares experienced their largest intraday drop since May after elevated labor and supply chain costs impacted quarterly margins, despite the retailer raising its annual sales and profit forecast. The company's efforts to mitigate supply chain disruptions, such as chartering its own vessels, were not enough to prevent a 42 basis point decline in gross margins for the third quarter. Analysts suggest these tight margins could persist through the holiday season, exacerbated by inflation and a labor shortage which forced Walmart to hire over 200,000 new workers.

feed.title: Amazon to stop accepting Visa's UK-issued credit cards over high fees
feed.summary: Amazon announced it will stop accepting UK-issued Visa credit cards from January 19, 2022, citing high transaction fees charged by Visa. While Visa debit cards and other credit card types like Mastercard and Amex will still be accepted, Visa expressed disappointment and hopes for a resolution. This move comes after Brexit removed an EU-enforced cap on card issuer fees in the UK, leading to calls for government intervention in UK-EU trade agreements and scrutiny of credit card fees from regulators.

feed.title: GXO Logistics targets North Texas in hiring spree to gear up for holiday e-commerce
feed.summary: GXO Logistics is expanding its workforce by hiring 650 additional workers in Dallas-Fort Worth to manage the expected surge in e-commerce during the holiday season. This local hiring spree is part of a larger effort to recruit 9,000 workers across the U.S. and Canada. The company is also boosting automation through increased robotics to enhance its supply chain efficiency.

feed.title: IIM Bangalore witnesses record summer placement season
feed.summary: IIM Bangalore's PGP and PGP-BA Class of 2021-23 recorded a successful summer placement season with 542 internship offers, placing all 513 students. Consulting companies led with 181 offers, followed by strong recruitment in IT, e-commerce, finance, and general management roles. The placements signal a reviving economy and high demand for talent to manage post-pandemic markets.

feed.title: Amazon (AMZN) in Talks to Switch Co-Brand Card to Mastercard (MA) From Visa (V)
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is reportedly in discussions to switch its co-branded credit card from Visa Inc. to Mastercard Inc. This potential move comes amidst ongoing disputes over payment costs, highlighted by Amazon's decision to ban Visa cards in the U.K. next year. The company is using the renewal talks for its significant co-branded card portfolio to negotiate more favorable terms with Visa.

feed.title: Credit crunch: Visa tumbles on Amazon ban in UK
feed.summary: Visa Inc. experienced a significant decline after Amazon.com Inc. announced it would stop accepting Visa credit cards issued in the U.K., citing high transaction fees. Amazon is also reportedly considering switching its co-branded credit card to Mastercard, further impacting Visa's stock and contributing to a 20% drop from its July record high. This move is part of broader international changes Amazon has made regarding Visa cards and comes amidst waxing investor sentiment towards credit card stocks due to the rise of "buy now, pay later" services.

feed.title: 3 REITs That Could Be the Backbone of Your Portfolio
feed.summary: This article highlights three REITs—Digital Realty Trust (DLR), STAG Industrial Inc (STAG), and Life Storage (LSI)—that could serve as strong core holdings in an investment portfolio. These REITs offer consistent income through dividends and provide exposure to robust real estate sectors like data centers, industrial properties, and self-storage, all benefiting from current market trends and offering reliability.

feed.title: Stock of the week - Rivian Automotive (18.11.2021)
feed.summary: Rivian Automotive, the US electric vehicles manufacturer, had its IPO on November 10th, with shares gaining 120% in the first 5 days before dropping 15%. Despite $0 revenue, the company achieved a significant market cap, briefly becoming the third largest automaker. Comparisons are drawn between Rivian's cash burn and early Tesla, with Rivian burning cash at a faster rate but benefiting from a massive order from Amazon.

feed.title: Starbucks links with Amazon Go for first cashier-less cafe
feed.summary: Starbucks has opened its first cashier-less cafe in New York City, in partnership with Amazon Go. This new format allows customers to order through the Starbucks app or use Amazon's checkout technology to grab food and drinks without a traditional cashier, addressing a U.S. labor crunch. Starbucks and Amazon plan to open at least two more such locations in 2022.

feed.title: Goldman Names Amazon As 2022 Top Internet Pick; Hails Uber, Lyft, Snap, Meta
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has named Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ: AMZN) as its top internet stock pick for 2022, assigning a Buy rating and a $4,100 price target. Analysts believe concerns about Amazon's revenue and profitability are already reflected in current prices, and the company benefits from broad secular growth themes. Other top picks include Uber Technologies Inc (NYSE: UBER), Lyft Inc (NASDAQ: LYFT), Snap Inc (NYSE: SNAP), and Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ: FB), which are seen as compelling buying opportunities due to recovery or as "mean reversion plays."

feed.title: Lehigh Valley warehouse offering up to $28 an hour for news hires amid labor shortage
feed.summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) is pushing to hire 160 more workers at its North Whitehall distribution and fulfillment center, offering wages up to $28 an hour and a competitive benefits package. A job fair is scheduled for November 19, and UNFI plans to include a wellness center, company store, café, video game room, and outdoor pavilion on-site. Other companies in the area, such as Uline and Amazon, are also struggling with labor shortages and are offering competitive wages and bonuses to attract workers.

feed.title: AWS Expands Access to Free Cloud Skills Training on its Mission to Educate 29 Million People by 2025
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has announced four new initiatives to expand access to free cloud computing skills training, aiming to educate 29 million people by 2025. These initiatives include the launch of AWS Skill Builder, the addition of AWS courses to Amazon.com, the expansion of the AWS re/Start reskilling program, and the opening of the AWS Skills Center. The move comes as a new AWS Global Digital Skills Study reveals that 85% of workers feel they need more technical knowledge post-pandemic, highlighting the growing demand for digital skills.

feed.title: Amazon to record $100m profit in Kornit secondary offering
feed.summary: Kornit Digital is conducting a secondary offering, benefiting Amazon, a significant shareholder. Amazon stands to make over $100 million in profit by selling a portion of its shares. Kornit plans to use the proceeds for potential acquisitions and general corporate purposes.

feed.title: Starbucks opens first cashierless cafe in NYC
feed.summary: Starbucks has opened its first cashierless cafe in New York City, utilizing Amazon's cashierless technology. This allows customers to order via the Starbucks app, grab their items, and leave without traditional payment. This move is part of Starbucks' strategy to shift towards smaller pickup locations and leverage automation, as 80% of its orders are on-the-go.

feed.title: Starbucks teams up with Amazon for new store concept
feed.summary: Starbucks and Amazon have partnered to open a new "Starbucks Pickup with Amazon Go" store concept in mid-town Manhattan, combining Starbucks' mobile pre-order service with Amazon's "Just Walk Out" cashierless technology. This innovative store allows customers to pre-order coffee through the Starbucks app and shop for food items using Amazon Go's frictionless checkout, with plans for at least two more locations in the coming year. The collaboration aims to offer customers convenience and a seamless experience, whether they are quickly picking up an order or staying to enjoy the lounge.

feed.title: Amazon and Starbucks partner on coffee shop with cashierless technology
feed.summary: Amazon and Starbucks have opened the first of three planned Starbucks Pickup with Amazon Go stores in New York City, marking their first collaborative effort. This new format integrates Starbucks' coffee shop experience with Amazon's cashierless technology. It is a partnership between the two Seattle-based companies to introduce a new retail concept.

feed.title: How Constellation Brands built a better ordering system on AWS
Why Sabre is betting against multi-cloud
feed.summary: Sabre, a travel technology company, has entered a 10-year, $2 billion partnership with Google Cloud, committing to a single cloud provider despite the prevailing multi-cloud trend among large enterprises. CIO Joe DiFonzo explains that an initial multi-cloud exploration led them to realize the inefficiencies of managing disparate architectures and the benefits of leveraging a single vendor's full capabilities and engineering mindset. This decision allows Sabre to focus on modernizing operations, decomposing applications into microservices, and integrating AI-powered services by leaning on Google's extensive technological advancements.

feed.title: The Amazon lobbyists who kill U.S. consumer privacy protections
feed.summary: This report details how Amazon has successfully lobbied against or weakened consumer privacy protections in over two dozen states, often by drafting its own industry-friendly legislation. Led by former White House aide Jay Carney, Amazon's lobbying efforts aim to preserve its access to vast amounts of consumer data, which fuels its e-commerce growth and advantages in emerging technologies like Alexa. The article uses internal documents and interviews to expose strategies such as "watering the flowers" to cultivate politicians and using job creation as leverage.

feed.title: Pentagon asks Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Oracle for bids on new cloud contracts
feed.summary: The Pentagon has requested bids from Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Oracle for new cloud contracts, moving away from a single-vendor approach. This initiative, known as Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability (JWCC), follows the cancellation of the highly contested JEDI contract previously awarded to Microsoft. While Amazon and Microsoft are expected to be primary awardees, the contracts aim to include other capable cloud service providers to support the Pentagon's diverse cloud computing needs.

feed.title: Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley Will Redesign AmEx Platinum Cards, and Other News
feed.summary: Artists Julie Mehretu and Kehinde Wiley are set to redesign the American Express U.S. Platinum Card, with the new designs launching next month during Art Basel Miami Beach. American Express also announced a $1 million donation to the Studio Museum in Harlem, supporting artists of African descent and aligning with its DEI initiatives. Other news includes Burberry's "Imagined Landscapes" pop-up in South Korea, the Preston Bus Station receiving the World Monuments Fund/Knoll Modernism Prize, and the renaming of Staples Center to Crypto.com Arena.

feed.title: Democratizing Education for All: Meet Anant Agarwal, edX Founder and 2U’s New Chief Open Education Officer
feed.summary: Anant Agarwal, founder of edX, has joined 2U as its first Chief Open Education Officer, aiming to further democratize education. Having witnessed global education disparities in his youth, Agarwal is passionate about making high-quality learning accessible to everyone. He envisions a modular, stackable education system that offers flexible, credential-based pathways to degrees, and believes the combined missions and product synergies of edX and 2U will create an unstoppable force for good in education.

feed.title: FedEx Sees 10% Rise in Holiday Package Volume to Record Levels
feed.summary: FedEx Corp. is forecasting a 10% increase in holiday package deliveries compared to an already record-breaking peak season in 2020, driven by continued consumer reliance on internet shopping. This volume represents 100 million more packages than pre-pandemic levels in 2019, with Cyber Monday alone expected to handle 32 million packages worldwide. Despite significant investments to handle the volume, the company faces challenges from worker shortages and supply chain congestion, which have increased costs by $450 million in its latest quarter.

feed.title: FedEx Opens Phoenix-Area Facility
feed.summary: FedEx Ground has opened its sixth facility in the Phoenix area, a 425,000-square-foot regional sortation center in Goodyear, Arizona. This expansion is part of a larger program to add 14.4 million square feet of sortation capacity for the holiday season, including new hubs, automated stations, and regional sortation facilities. The new facility is located in Prologis Commerce Park at Goodyear, a key industrial park contributing to the West Valley's booming distribution scene, driven by the e-commerce surge.

feed.title: Medtronic partners with Amazon to bring the PillCam to your door
feed.summary: Medtronic has partnered with Amazon to deliver its PillCam SB 3 Capsule Endoscopy System directly to patients' homes, enabling remote endoscopy particularly in rural areas. This collaboration, which accelerated due to the pandemic, allows doctors to order PillCams through a special Amazon portal, marking Amazon's continued expansion into the healthcare sector and leveraging its logistics capabilities. The partnership highlights the growing trend of telehealth and in-home medical device use, with Medtronic planning to expand its at-home gastrointestinal care offerings.

feed.title: Online retailer Wayfair looks to hire 200 tech employees for new Seattle satellite office
feed.summary: Online retailer Wayfair plans to hire 200 tech employees for a new satellite office in Seattle. The company, which specializes in home goods and furniture, will begin hiring immediately, with employees starting remotely. Seattle is one of four locations, including Toronto, the Bay Area, and Austin, Texas, where Wayfair plans to establish satellite offices.

feed.title: Accenture to help build Organon’s enterprise capabilities with SAP S4/HANA on AWS
feed.summary: Accenture has been chosen by Organon to lead the transformation of its enterprise resource planning (ERP) technology into a cloud-based digital core using SAP S/4HANA on AWS. This initiative aims to standardize Organon's technology platform, integrate business processes, provide real-time analytics, and improve patient and employee experiences. Accenture will leverage its accelerators and myConcerto® platform to guide the implementation and foster continuous innovation for Organon's future growth.

feed.title: Cannabis company High Tide eyes more acquisitions as CEO channels Jeff Bezos for e-commerce inspiration
feed.summary: High Tide CEO Raj Grover is looking to Amazon's Jeff Bezos for inspiration as the cannabis company plans its largest acquisition to date. Grover aims to build up an online cannabis marketplace, focusing on e-commerce for CBD and cannabis products. He stated that Bezos is an idol and his approach to direct-to-consumer business stems from Bezos's success in changing the world.

feed.title: Holiday Shoppers Can Buy Texas Roadhouse Steaks Online for First Time
feed.summary: Due to a significant decrease in the national turkey inventory and related supply chain issues, Texas Roadhouse is offering its hand-trimmed steaks for online purchase for the first time. Shoppers can buy USDA Choice steaks, aged a minimum of 28 days, directly from trbutchershop.com or Amazon, complete with seasoning and cooking instructions. This initiative aims to provide a high-quality alternative for holiday meals amidst turkey shortages.

feed.title: Walmart Takes New Step Toward Making Aerial Deliveries Routine
feed.summary: Walmart is expanding its aerial delivery efforts by partnering with DroneUp to deliver household items in Arkansas, with plans to extend service to other locations. While routine drone deliveries are still years away due to regulatory hurdles, the collaboration highlights the retail industry's commitment to this new delivery method amid the growth of online shopping. Other major companies like Alphabet, Amazon, and UPS are also developing similar drone delivery programs.

feed.title: New SAFESKIN* Nitrile Gloves in POP-N-GO* Packs Provide On-The-Go Protection
feed.summary: Owens & Minor has launched a new consumer brand, SAFESKIN*, featuring Nitrile Gloves in innovative POP-N-GO* packs. These gloves are designed for convenience and portability, offering task-focused protection for light, medium, and heavy-duty tasks. The product aims to provide individuals with reliable protection against germs, chemicals, dirt, and messes in various settings.

feed.title: Observing Attacks Against Hundreds of Exposed Services in Public Clouds
feed.summary: Unit 42 researchers deployed a global honeypot infrastructure of 320 nodes to study attacks against exposed services in public clouds. They found that 80% of honeypots were compromised within 24 hours and all within a week, highlighting the rapid pace at which attackers exploit misconfigured services. The research emphasizes the ineffectiveness of IP-based firewalls and the critical need for quick vulnerability remediation in cloud environments.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble Buys Farmacy Beauty
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble has acquired Farmacy Beauty, a cult "farm-to-face skincare" brand, to expand its skincare portfolio and attract younger consumers. P&G aims to leverage Farmacy's science-based sustainable products and D2C presence, along with its existing retail channels like Sephora, QVC, and Amazon. The deal is expected to help Farmacy grow beyond its current $80 million net sales, with brand founder David Chung remaining as an advisor and Mina Chae becoming the new President and CEO.

feed.title: Amazon and Apple handed $225 million in Italian antitrust fines
feed.summary: Italy's antitrust authority has fined Amazon and Apple a total of more than 200 million euros ($225 million) for alleged anti-competitive cooperation. The fines stem from a 2018 agreement that restricted the sale of Apple and Beats products on Amazon.it to only selected resellers, violating EU rules and impacting price competition. Both companies deny wrongdoing and plan to appeal the decision.

feed.title: Cut your cables with the Onn Roku Smart TV for $128 today
feed.summary: Walmart is offering the 40-inch Onn Roku Smart TV for $128, providing a budget-friendly option for cutting cable expenses. This smart TV allows users to access free and paid content from platforms like Netflix and Hulu directly, requiring only a Wi-Fi connection. It includes a Roku remote, TV stand, and HDR capabilities for 1080P Full High Definition viewing.

feed.title: 20-Year Returns: Monster Eats Apple for Breakfast
feed.summary: Over the past 20 years, Monster Beverage Corporation's stock has outperformed major tech companies like Apple and Amazon, increasing by over 100,000 percent. The company, an early entrant in the energy drink market, has grown significantly, recording $1.4 billion in profit on $4.6 billion in sales in 2020. Monster Beverage is now a dominant player in the energy drink market and has recently been in merger talks with Constellation Brands.

feed.title: Urban Outfitters Sees Q3 Sales Boost Despite Sluggish Store Traffic
feed.summary: Urban Outfitters reported record Q3 net sales of $1.13 billion, an almost 17% increase year-over-year, and exceeded Wall Street's earnings projections. Despite this, the stock price fell due to declining brick-and-mortar store traffic, which was down in the negative mid-teens on a two-year basis. The company attributed some inventory increases to a strategic decision to mitigate supply chain disruptions and anticipates mid-teens comparable retail segment sales growth for Q4.

feed.title: The Kodak Reelz Digitizer Converts 8mm Film Strips into MP4 Files
feed.summary: C+A Global, a licensee of Eastman Kodak, has launched the Kodak Reelz Film Digitizer, which converts 8mm and Super 8 film strips into 1080p Full HD MP4 files. The device features a five-inch viewing screen, easy-to-use controls for editing sharpness and tint, and a fully automated 8.08-megapixel sensor. It's designed to be ultra-compact and is available on Amazon for $400.

feed.title: Company profile: Iron Mountain
feed.summary: This article profiles Iron Mountain, an enterprise information management company and data center provider. It details the company's history, starting from its founding in 1951 by Herman Knaust as a bomb-resistant storage facility in a former iron ore mine. The profile highlights its evolution, expansion, and its commitment to sustainability, including being a signatory of the Amazon Climate Pledge.

feed.title: Target unveils Cyber Monday sale with two days of online deals and 'Holiday Best' sale
feed.summary: Target announced its Cyber Monday sale for two days of online deals and also introduced a weeklong "Holiday Best" deals sale. The "Holiday Best" deals signify Target's lowest planned prices for the season and are backed by an enhanced price-match guarantee through December 24. These sales complement Adobe's projections for Cyber Monday as the year's biggest shopping day, urging consumers to look for discounts on a wide range of products including electronics, home goods, and apparel.

feed.title: Black Friday boosts some OKC area stores, while other retailers see less foot traffic
feed.summary: Black Friday sales in the Oklahoma City area in 2021 saw a significant shift due to the rise of online shopping and extended discount periods. While some specialty stores like Academy Sports + Outdoors experienced good foot traffic, many larger retailers like Walmart and Sam's Club noted a much calmer in-store experience compared to previous years. This change is largely attributed to retailers offering early Black Friday deals and enhanced online shopping options, with some popular items selling out online before traditional in-store events.

feed.title: Yassir raises $30 million in Series A
feed.summary: Algerian super app Yassir has successfully raised $30 million in a Series A funding round, led by several prominent investors including WndrCo and DN Capital. The company, founded in 2016, offers ride-hailing, parcel, and on-demand delivery services across Algeria, Morocco, and Tunisia, serving three million users. This investment will primarily be used to expand its operations into sub-Saharan Africa and hire more local tech talent, with a strategic focus on empowering local communities and potentially offering payment services for unbanked populations.

feed.title: Gilead and AWS Collaborate on Development and Delivery of New Medicines for Patients
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences has selected Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred cloud provider to accelerate the development and delivery of new medicines. By utilizing AWS's machine learning and analytics capabilities, Gilead aims to enhance clinical trial design, improve data-driven decision-making, and refine its drug pipeline while prioritizing patient privacy. Additionally, Gilead is migrating its enterprise resource planning (ERP) workloads to AWS to implement SAP S/4HANA across its global operations, leading to increased efficiency, agility, and a more data-driven business model.

feed.title: Corsair TX650M Power Supply Review: Quietly Good
feed.summary: This article reviews the Corsair TX650M power supply, highlighting its high-quality construction, satisfactory performance, and quiet operation at a fair price. While it offers full power at 47 degrees Celsius, good load regulation, and a 7-year warranty, its transient response and semi-modular design are noted as areas for improvement. The review provides detailed specifications and a component analysis, affirming its position as a solid choice for its price point despite minor drawbacks.

feed.title: FedEx, UPS Turn Holiday Surge Into Someone Else’s Problem
feed.summary: During the holiday season, major carriers FedEx and UPS are reportedly turning away lower-yielding business and raising prices, placing the burden of increased package volumes on smaller regional couriers. These smaller operators are struggling with issues like transportation logjams and worker shortages, which may lead to delayed deliveries and higher costs for consumers. Despite efforts by regional couriers and the U.S. Postal Service to boost capacity, the overall delivery network faces significant challenges, urging some shoppers towards alternative purchasing options.

feed.title: Amazon Braket adds support for hybrid quantum-classical algorithms
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services Inc. has introduced Amazon Braket Hybrid Jobs, a new feature for its quantum computing cloud service. This feature aims to simplify the process of setting up, monitoring, and executing hybrid quantum-classical algorithms, which combine classical computing to optimize quantum algorithms. By offering a fully managed service and priority access to quantum processing units, Amazon seeks to make quantum computing more accessible and predictable for developers.

feed.title: KKR, here is who and what is behind the 460 mln $ giant who wants to buy Italy’s telecom group TIM
feed.summary: This article details the key Italian figures within the US alternative investment giant KKR, particularly in light of its proposed acquisition of the Italian telecommunications group TIM. It highlights Mattia Caprioli, KKR's co-head of European Private Equity, and Alberto Signori, a managing director specializing in infrastructure deals, who was involved in the FiberCop deal with TIM and Fastweb, a precursor to the current offer. The article also mentions Diego Piacentini, KKR's senior advisor for technology, media, and telecommunications, and provides an overview of KKR's global infrastructure investments and its significant assets under management.

feed.title: Amazon has floated the idea of buying HubSpot, says report
feed.summary: A report indicates that Amazon has considered acquiring HubSpot, one of the largest public companies in Massachusetts. This potential acquisition would involve Amazon, one of the state's largest employers, expanding its presence through a significant M&A deal. The article highlights related news concerning HubSpot's leadership, funding, and real estate.

feed.title: WPP acquires Cloud Commerce Group
feed.summary: WPP has acquired Cloud Commerce Group (CCG), a UK-based technology company specializing in helping brands market, sell, and deliver products across global e-commerce platforms like Amazon and eBay. CCG's platform manages over £1 billion in annual gross merchandise value and will join Wunderman Thompson's network, strengthening WPP's commerce offerings and contributing to its growth strategy. This acquisition aims to enhance WPP's ability to assist clients with complex omnichannel commerce needs.

feed.title: AIG Selects AWS as Its Preferred Public Cloud Provider
