feed.summary: American International Group (AIG) has selected Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred public cloud provider to drive business transformation, increase operational efficiency, and accelerate innovation. This move is part of AIG's "AIG 200" strategy, aiming to improve core business processes, modernize technology, and enhance customer experiences for its 90 million policyholders worldwide. AIG's digital transformation with AWS began in 2017, and the expanded relationship in 2021 and 2022 will further accelerate their adoption of cloud services.

feed.title: Amazon's cloud unit launches new chips to take on Intel, Nvidia
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has introduced two new custom computing chips, Graviton3 and Trainium, aimed at competing with offerings from Intel, AMD, and Nvidia. AWS claims its new chips offer better performance per dollar and lower costs for machine learning model training compared to its rivals. This move signals AWS's continued drive to develop its own hardware and intensify competition in the cloud computing and chip markets.

feed.title: Glass Sands wind farm goes online in southern Oklahoma
feed.summary: Southern Power has announced that its 118-megawatt Glass Sands Wind Facility in Murray County, Oklahoma, is now operational. This marks Southern Power's fifth wind facility in Oklahoma and its 15th overall, contributing to the company's expanding clean energy portfolio. The electricity and renewable energy credits from the 28 Vestas wind turbines will be sold to Amazon under a power purchase agreement.

feed.title: Meta Selects AWS as Key, Long-Term Strategic Cloud Provider
feed.summary: Meta has deepened its relationship with Amazon Web Services (AWS) as a strategic cloud provider, leveraging AWS's infrastructure for research and development, third-party collaborations, and operational efficiency. The companies will also work together to optimize PyTorch performance on AWS, facilitating faster and easier development, training, deploying, and operating of AI/ML models for enterprises. This collaboration aims to help organizations bring large-scale deep learning models from research to production more efficiently on AWS.

feed.title: The FAANG Market Is Fading
feed.summary: The article discusses the fading hyper-concentration of stock market returns from a handful of large technology stocks, particularly the FAANGs, in 2021 compared to 2020. This shift suggests a healthier market dynamic with less risk for index funds and more opportunities for stock-pickers. While large stocks still contribute significantly, their overall impact on market returns has decreased, resembling a more traditional market distribution.

feed.title: The curious case of the Ubiquiti employee-whistleblower-hacker
feed.summary: This article details the peculiar case of Nikolas Sharp, a former Ubiquiti employee who allegedly hacked the company's systems, stole data, and demanded a $2 million ransom in Bitcoin. After being exposed due to an IP logging error during the hack, Sharp reportedly attempted to cover his tracks by reaching out to news organizations as a whistleblower, claiming Ubiquiti downplayed the breach's severity. Sharp faces charges including hacking, wire fraud, and extortion, carrying a potential sentence of up to 37 years in prison.

feed.title: What Stocks Are in QQQ? Rebound Likely, Good Long-Term Pick
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq 100, is a tech-heavy fund that experienced a nosedive following the omicron variant news and inflation concerns in December 2021. Despite this slump, the article suggests that QQQ is likely to recover due to its diverse holdings and past performance, making it a potentially smart long-term investment. The article details the top 20 holdings and the industry breakdown of the ETF.

feed.title: ProShares CEO Sapir Named on 2021 Bloomberg 50 List
feed.summary: ProShares CEO Michael L. Sapir has been recognized on the 2021 Bloomberg 50 list for his contributions to the business world, particularly for leading the launch of the ProShares Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITO). BITO became the fastest ETF to reach $1 billion in assets under management in just two days. This achievement highlights ProShares' ongoing innovation in the ETF space under Sapir's leadership, including previously launching the first leveraged and inverse ETFs and the first pet care industry ETF in the U.S.

feed.title: Salesforce uses new infrastructure architecture Hyperforce to help government manage pandemic scaling needs
feed.summary: Salesforce has introduced Hyperforce, a new infrastructure architecture designed to help governmental bodies manage the rapidly scaling digital demands brought on by the pandemic. This architecture allows for flexible scaling and deployment in new regions, crucial for efforts like COVID tracing, testing, and vaccine management. Salesforce's Government Cloud Plus offering, which is FedRAMP and HIPAA compliant, streamlines security and protocol adherence for public sector clients.

feed.title: Adopt a data-centric approach to cyber security
feed.summary: This article emphasizes the need for organizations to shift from traditional perimeter-based security to a data-centric approach for cybersecurity. It highlights the importance of discovering and classifying data, implementing a layered security framework, and balancing security with usability. The article also discusses the advantages of cloud-native architectures and emerging AI-based tools for enhancing data protection against evolving cyber threats.

feed.title: Problems at Redwire (RDW)
feed.summary: Redwire (NYSE: RDW), a space equipment manufacturer, went public through a SPAC merger at a $620 million valuation, despite acquiring its underlying companies for approximately $150 million. The author highlights concerns about aggressive SPAC projections, questionable metrics like a largely cancellable backlog, and an inability to file its first 10-Q, suggesting the company is overvalued.

feed.title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
feed.summary: An article discussing the importance of balancing tech investments with high-quality stocks. It highlights Mark Mahaney's "dislocated high-quality" (DHQ) investment tenet and introduces the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as a suitable option for investors seeking quality and reduced volatility.

feed.title: Netflix, Disney Sue Streaming Sites for Copyright Infringement
feed.summary: Netflix, Disney, and several other major entertainment companies have filed a lawsuit against two streaming sites, AllAccessTV (AATV) and Quality Restreams, alleging copyright infringement. The companies claim that these sites are illegally selling access to their copyrighted movies and television programs without permission. The lawsuit was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California and includes plaintiffs such as Universal Studios, Paramount Pictures, Amazon, Apple, and Warner Bros.

feed.title: Is there a new food market opening across South Florida? What to know about Sprouts
feed.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market is rapidly expanding its presence across Florida, with several new stores opening in late 2021 and early 2022, including locations in Dania Beach, Boynton Beach, West Palm Beach, West Kendall, and Oakland Park. The Phoenix-based chain, which focuses on fresh produce and health-conscious products, aims to grow its Florida footprint to 31 stores by January, supported by a new Orlando distribution center and local produce buyers. Sprouts emphasizes unique healthy products, bulk goods, and a distinct shopping experience, and its expansion puts it in direct competition with established Florida grocers like Publix.

feed.title: HP Envy 7640 review
feed.summary: The HP Envy 7640 is an affordable all-in-one color printer designed for home or small office use, featuring easy setup, Wi-Fi Direct, and compatibility with HP's Instant Ink service. While it offers a good range of features like dual paper trays and automatic two-sided printing, its print quality is only average, especially for photos, and it suffered from occasional paper jams during testing. The review concludes that it's a practical choice for general printing tasks but not recommended for those prioritizing high-quality photo output.

feed.title: Deloitte buys Oracle Cloud specialist BIAS Corporation
feed.summary: Deloitte US has acquired BIAS Corporation, an IT consultancy specializing in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), to enhance its cloud migration capabilities. BIAS, founded in 2000, has over 350 employees and was recognized as an Oracle Global and North American Partner of the Year. This acquisition addresses the increasing demand for cloud services and follows Deloitte's fiscal year 2021 revenue growth to over $50 billion.

feed.title: Market Expert: Fall For Quality, Not Just Tech
feed.summary: A market expert suggests investors prioritize high-quality stocks over solely focusing on big tech names. Mark Mahaney of Evercore ISI advocates for investing in "dislocated high-quality" companies with large total addressable markets and strong innovation, rather than just trading tech stocks. The article highlights the FlexShares US Quality Low Volatility Index Fund (QLV) as an example of an ETF that focuses on quality while managing volatility.

feed.title: Great Outdoors Group and Sportsman’s Warehouse Holdings Cancel Merger Plans
feed.summary: Great Outdoors Group, LLC and Sportsman’s Warehouse Holdings, Inc. have canceled their proposed $785 million merger following an 11-month investigation by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC). The FTC's Bureau of Competition Director, Holly Vedova, stated that the merger would have harmed consumers through increased prices, reduced product offerings, and diminished quality and service. Both companies specialize in outdoor sporting goods, and the FTC found that their competition benefited customers in at least two dozen local markets across the U.S.

feed.title: Postmates spin-off Serve Robotics raises $13M to grow its sidewalk delivery fleet
feed.summary: Serve Robotics, a spin-off from Postmates, has successfully raised $13 million in an expanded seed funding round with significant participation from Uber, Delivery Hero, and 7-Eleven's venture arm. This capital injection will allow Serve Robotics to expand its commercial operations, grow its team, and further develop its technology platform. The company aims to deploy its sidewalk delivery robots in every major U.S. city within the next two to three years, building on its existing record of tens of thousands of deliveries in Los Angeles and San Francisco.

feed.title: The Future Of Energy: Using Digital Twins As A Strategic Asset At GE Digital
feed.summary: GE Digital is leveraging digital twins and machine learning on AWS to help customers in the industrial sector optimize operations, reduce maintenance costs, and achieve sustainability goals. This approach enables remote monitoring, predictive insights, and streamlined workflows, significantly improving efficiency and reducing carbon emissions. The company emphasizes the importance of simplifying complexity, fostering collaboration among digital twins, and securing executive buy-in for successful digital transformation.

feed.title: US-Based Equinix Acquires Nigerian Digital Infrastructure Firm MainOne for $320m
feed.summary: US-based Equinix Inc. is acquiring Nigerian digital infrastructure firm MainOne Cable Co Ltd. for $320 million, marking Equinix's first entry into the African market. MainOne, founded by Funke Opeke, operates three data centers with a fourth under construction, alongside plans for a sub-sea cable connecting Portugal to several West African nations. This acquisition positions Equinix to capitalize on the rapidly growing demand for data use and storage capacity in Africa, while providing MainOne with crucial capital and network access for its expansion.

feed.title: 5 Free Resources for Tech Learning Include AWS, Coursera, More
feed.summary: This article highlights five free resources for individuals looking to enhance their tech skills: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Coursera, Microsoft Learn, NPower, and Per Scholas. It advises technologists to have clear goals when choosing a program, distinguishing between learning new skills and securing a new job. The resources offer various learning styles, from self-paced online courses to intensive bootcamps, and some provide industry-recognized credentials.

feed.title: DXC partners with AWS to bring personalized cloud solutions to the enterprise
feed.summary: DXC Technology Co. and Amazon Web Services Inc. have partnered to offer personalized cloud transformation solutions for businesses. This collaboration, highlighted during AWS re:Invent, focuses on guiding companies from existing data centers to cloud adoption through DXC's "Cloud Right" strategy. The approach emphasizes tailoring solutions to individual customer needs, resulting in significant operational cost reductions for clients, such as a leading insurance company that saw a 30% budget decrease.

feed.title: Amazon Web Services outage brings some delivery operations to a standstill
feed.summary: An Amazon Web Services (AWS) outage on Tuesday affected various websites and services, including Disney+, Netflix, and Coinbase, as well as critical internal Amazon tools like its Flex and AtoZ apps. The outage, which primarily impacted the AWS US-East-1 region, also brought some Amazon delivery operations to a standstill, causing significant disruptions during the crucial "peak season" for holiday shoppers. While many underlying issues were mitigated by Tuesday evening, some services continued to experience problems.

feed.title: Major Outage Strikes Amazon Web Services ; Netflix, Venmo, Instacart Among Many Affected Sites
feed.summary: A major Amazon Web Services (AWS) outage on December 7, 2021, disrupted numerous popular sites and services, including Netflix, Venmo, and Instacart, and even affected Amazon's own delivery operations. The six-hour disruption, primarily in the eastern U.S., highlighted the growing reliance on centralized cloud services and the cascading impact when they fail, leaving customers and students without access to essential online platforms. Experts noted that such outages are becoming more common due to the centralization and automation of the internet, contrasting with its original design for decentralization.

feed.title: How 2U Is Helping Nestlé Purina Increase the Diversity of Its Tech Talent Pool
feed.summary: Nestlé Purina has partnered with 2U through its Project Opportunity initiative to diversify its tech talent pool by tapping into underserved communities such as military veterans and women. The partnership helps Nestlé Purina find tech professionals with skills in areas like machine learning and data analysis, while also providing career readiness and opportunities to boot camp students. This collaboration aims to foster a more inclusive workforce and benefit from a wider range of ideas and strengths.

feed.title: Amazon’s Prime Video, apps down for thousands of users
feed.summary: Amazon’s Prime Video and other services, including applications using Amazon Web Services (AWS), experienced significant outages for thousands of users in the United States. The company attributed the disruption to network devices and an application programming interface (API), which also affected its ability to provide timely updates. Other services like Ring security cameras, Chime, and iRobot were also impacted, highlighting the widespread dependency on AWS infrastructure.

feed.title: Amazon Cloud Outage Hits Major Websites, Streaming Apps
feed.summary: A major Amazon cloud services outage impacted several prominent websites and streaming apps, including Netflix, Disney+, Robinhood, and Amazon's own e-commerce site, as consumers were shopping for Christmas. Amazon attributed the disruption to network devices and application programming interface issues, with many services beginning to recover later in the day. This incident follows a similar widespread outage earlier in the year that affected numerous major websites.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Ingram Micro Acquires Brlink, Strengthening Its Partner Ecosystem with New Managed Services
feed.summary: Ingram Micro has acquired BRLink, a leading managed services provider in Brazil, to enhance its cloud services portfolio and strengthen its partner ecosystem. This acquisition positions Ingram Micro as the sole distributor in Brazil capable of offering complete cloud migration services, leveraging BRLink's expertise in managed services, data analysis, and artificial intelligence. BRLink will continue to operate as a business unit within Ingram Micro Brazil, led by its founder Rafael Marangoni, supporting Ingram Micro's growth in the rapidly expanding Latin American cloud market.

feed.title: Zscaler Extends its Proven Zero Trust Exchange Platform to Deliver Zero Trust for Workloads
feed.summary: Zscaler announced the general availability of its new Workload Communications solution, extending Zero Trust security to workloads and applications in public clouds. This solution aims to simplify multi-cloud workload connectivity, eliminate attack surfaces, and enhance application performance by moving away from traditional VPNs and firewalls. It provides Zero Trust for cloud workloads across cloud-to-internet, cloud-to-cloud, cloud-to-data center, and intra-cloud communications.

feed.title: How much control should Apple have over your iPhone and the App Store?
feed.summary: Apple is facing increasing scrutiny over its tight control of the App Store, which critics call a monopoly due to its exclusive app distribution, mandatory commissions, and content restrictions. While Apple argues these measures ensure user security and privacy, lawmakers and regulators worldwide are pushing for changes to increase competition, potentially leading to more app choices and lower prices for consumers, but also raising concerns about security. Despite some minor concessions, Apple largely maintains its stance, suggesting significant shifts may only occur if forced by new legislation or legal challenges.

feed.title: B2C E-commerce Market May Set a New Epic Growth Story |
feed.summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics titled "B2C E-commerce Market Insights, to 2026" forecasts significant growth for the B2C e-commerce market, driven by increasing disposable income, global per capita income, and expanding internet penetration. Key players in this evolving market include Alibaba, Amazon, eBay, and Apple. The report details market trends, opportunities, drivers, and challenges, segmenting the market by type, application, brand, and product across various regions.

feed.title: FAA makes its ruling on the height of Amazon’s Helix tower at HQ2
feed.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has concluded its review of Amazon's proposed 355-foot-tall Helix building at its HQ2 PenPlace site. The FAA's decision follows an almost eight-month review process regarding the height of the unique conical structure. Arlington officials have previously expressed desires for more green elements in Amazon's PenPlace development, and there was earlier concern from airports authority regarding the Helix's height.

feed.title: Sonos now supports Dolby Atmos and 24-bit hi-res audio on Amazon Music
feed.summary: Sonos has expanded its audio support to include Amazon Music Unlimited's hi-res and Dolby Atmos music. This allows Amazon Music subscribers to stream "Ultra HD" (24-bit) tracks and Dolby Atmos content on compatible Sonos devices. While Amazon offers up to 24-bit/192kHz, Sonos devices will stream up to 24-bit/48kHz, with broader compatibility for hi-res than for Dolby Atmos which is limited to the Beam Gen 2 and Arc.

feed.title: SparrowHawk’s 255 Logistics Center in St. Louis now fully leased
feed.summary: SparrowHawk LLC has announced that its 502,800-square-foot 255 Logistics Center in St. Louis is now fully leased after securing two significant transactions. The latest lease is with a large e-commerce firm for 202,300 square feet, while Sensient Technologies Corporation leased the remaining 300,481 square feet for relocation and expansion. These deals are among the largest industrial transactions in the St. Louis metro area for 2021.

feed.title: NFL's Raiders use analytics to engage new customer base
feed.summary: The NFL's Las Vegas Raiders modernized their analytics stack to understand and engage a new customer base after moving from Oakland. They partnered with Data Clymer to build a cloud-based system using Salesforce, Matillion, Snowflake, and Tableau to process and analyze customer data. This new system allows the Raiders to rapidly gain insights, develop targeted marketing campaigns, and maximize diverse revenue opportunities from their new stadium in Las Vegas, which operates as a tourist destination.

feed.title: Tecnoglass stock plummets as short-seller alleges cartel connections
feed.summary: Tecnoglass stock plummeted over 40% after Hindenburg Research, a short-seller, accused the Colombia-based glassmaker of having ties to the infamous Cali cartel and operating on fraudulent figures. The report details past allegations against the company's founders, Jose and Christian Daes, including involvement with weapon smuggling, cocaine trafficking, and money laundering for the cartel, as well as alleged non-disclosure of related-party transactions. Hindenburg Research, which has taken a bet against the stock, suspects a significant portion of Tecnoglass' revenue is faked.

feed.title: Vita Coco duo launch non-HFSS cereal brand Surreal
feed.summary: Two former Vita Coco executives, Kit Gammell and Jac Chetland, have launched a new non-HFSS (high in fat, sugar, and salt) cereal brand called Surreal. The brand offers four sugar-free, high-protein, low-carb flavors and aims to disrupt the traditional cereal market by being nutritionally relevant for adults while retaining the fun of childhood cereals. Surreal has attracted significant investors and plans a sampling campaign before launching on Amazon and DTC channels in January.

feed.title: Kroger and Ocado to build automated e-commerce site in North Carolina
feed.summary: Kroger plans to build a 200,000-square-foot automated customer fulfillment center (CFC) in Concord, North Carolina, in partnership with Ocado. This facility is expected to open within two years, create up to 700 jobs, and use unmanned vehicles to efficiently process grocery orders. The new CFC will enhance Kroger's e-commerce agility and capitalize on incentives from North Carolina, including a potential $2.3 million reimbursement.

feed.title: 10 Gym Bags That Have Shoe Compartments Built Right In
feed.summary: This article highlights the convenience of gym bags with dedicated shoe compartments, preventing dirty shoes from mixing with clean clothes. It features a curated list of 10 such bags, ranging from duffels to backpacks, with options for various budgets and needs. Each bag is described with its key features, pricing, and where to purchase it.

feed.title: Starbucks workers vote to unionize at Buffalo, New York, store
feed.summary: Starbucks employees at one store in Buffalo, New York, have voted to join a union, marking the coffee chain's first unionized company-owned location in the U.S. This outcome, though involving a small number of workers, is seen as symbolically significant and could encourage further unionization efforts across other Starbucks cafes and companies like Amazon. A second store rejected unionization, and a third store's results are still pending review.

feed.title: America’s Largest Retail Union Calls on CEOs for Action on Omicron and Winter COVID Surge to Protect Essential Workers and Customers
feed.summary: The United Food and Commercial Workers (UFCW), America's largest retail and food union, has urged top U.S. retail and grocery CEOs to take immediate action to protect employees and customers against the Omicron variant and the winter COVID surge. The union's letter highlighted increased worker deaths and infections, proposing five key measures for companies to implement. These measures include promoting mask-wearing, providing free PPE, re-establishing social distancing, supporting worker vaccinations with paid leave, and implementing inflation wage protection.
feed.source: The United Food & Commercial Workers International Union
feed.source_domain: The United Food & Commercial Workers International Union

feed.title: Starbucks can afford 'decent wage with decent benefits,' says Sanders as N.Y. store unionizes
feed.summary: Workers at a Starbucks store in Buffalo, N.Y., voted to unionize on Thursday, marking a first for the 50-year-old coffee chain in the U.S. This development signals a potential resurgence of the labour movement, with Senator Bernie Sanders advocating for better wages and benefits for Starbucks employees, citing the CEO's substantial compensation. The vote's certification is pending any objections, but it represents a symbolic victory for labour, potentially influencing other Starbucks locations and fast-food chains.

feed.title: Apache Log4j Vulnerability - Check Point Blog
feed.summary: This article from Check Point Research details the critical Log4j vulnerability (CVE-2021-44228), a remote code execution exploit impacting a vast number of Java-based applications globally. It explains the nature of the vulnerability, the rapid evolution of exploit variations, and the immediate protective measures offered by Check Point's Infinity Platform. The piece also confirms that Check Point's own architecture is not affected and provides guidance on mitigation and continued support.

feed.title: Six dead, no hope of more survivors after tornadoes destroy Amazon warehouse
feed.summary: Six Amazon workers were confirmed dead after a series of tornadoes caused a warehouse near St. Louis to collapse. Authorities have shifted from rescue to recovery efforts, with little hope of finding more survivors. The company and Illinois' governor have pledged support, while the incident highlights safety concerns during severe weather.

feed.title: Healthcare Triangle (HCTI) Acquires DevCool, an Electronic
feed.summary: Healthcare Triangle (HCTI) announced the acquisition of DevCool, an EHR-focused healthcare IT and managed services company, a move expected to add approximately $20 million in revenues. This acquisition aims to strengthen HCTI's leadership in healthcare IT by improving patient care, reducing IT costs, and accelerating EHR deployments, especially for cloud hosting. The combined entity plans to leverage cloud technologies to enhance clinical, operational, and financial performance for healthcare providers and integrate DevCool’s client base with HCTI’s SaaS platforms.

feed.title: Harley to take EV unit public via SPAC deal as valuations soar
feed.summary: Harley-Davidson's electric-motorcycle division, LiveWire, will go public through a merger with a blank-check firm, AEA-Bridges Impact Corp., in a deal valued at $1.77 billion. This move aims to attract younger customers and capitalize on the soaring valuations of electric-vehicle makers. Harley-Davidson will retain a 74% stake in LiveWire, which is expected to list on the New York Stock Exchange.

feed.title: ZeroAvia Raises Further $35 Million for Zero-Emission Flight Technology, Taking Total Raised to $115 Million
feed.summary: ZeroAvia has successfully raised an additional $35 million, bringing its total investment to $115 million. This funding round includes new investors like Alaska Airlines and United Airlines, alongside existing backers such as Amazon’s Climate Pledge Fund and Breakthrough Energy Ventures. The capital will accelerate the development of ZeroAvia’s 2-5MW zero-emission powertrain system, targeting regional aircraft by 2026 and regional jets by 2028, as the company moves closer to commercializing its hydrogen-electric technology.

feed.title: 10 Technology Vendors Affected By The Log4j Vulnerability
feed.summary: The critical Log4j vulnerability has impacted numerous technology vendors, including prominent names like Cisco, IBM, VMware, and MSP-focused companies such as ConnectWise and N-able. This zero-day exploit allows attackers to execute arbitrary code due to vulnerable Java logging package Log4j, prompting vendors to rapidly develop patches and workarounds, though some fixes may not be available until early 2022. Several companies, including Amazon Web Services, Broadcom, Fortinet, HCL, and Okta, have issued advisories and remediation steps for their affected products, warning customers to update or implement temporary mitigations.

feed.title: Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings Appoints Mario Sampson SVP of Supply Chain
feed.summary: Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings, Inc. announced the appointment of Mario Sampson as Senior Vice President of Supply Chain, reporting to Eric van der Valk. Sampson will oversee domestic and international transportation, logistics, and distribution centers, bringing extensive experience from previous roles at Unique Industries, Macy's, Amazon, and Target. CEO John Swygert highlighted Sampson's strong leadership and proven track record in driving process enhancements, anticipating his contributions to the company's long-term growth.

feed.title: 29 Things You Should Never Buy at Walmart
feed.summary: Walmart, a retail giant known for its aggressive pursuit of low prices, might not always be the best option for certain purchases due to quality issues or better deals elsewhere. This article identifies 29 items, ranging from electronics and appliances to groceries and personal care products, that consumers might be better off buying from other retailers. The recommendations are based on research from various consumer-focused publications, emphasizing that "you get what you pay for."

feed.title: Opting for a gift with a personal touch this holiday Christmas? Make sure to order now
feed.summary: Personalized and handmade gifts are popular choices for the holidays, but shoppers need to order them well in advance due to longer creation and shipping times. Companies like Etsy and Framebridge recommend early shopping, though they also offer options for last-minute gift-givers such as "Ready to Ship" items or quick-ship frames. Delivery times can vary significantly depending on the customization required for the item.

feed.title: Mesa Airlines adding freighter for DHL service as cargo ambitions grow
feed.summary: Mesa Airlines is expanding its cargo operations for DHL Express by adding another narrowbody freighter to its fleet in early 2022, following a fiscal fourth-quarter loss of $7.5 million. The regional passenger airline aims to grow its cargo fleet to eight to ten jets to improve efficiency and profitability, leveraging the increasing demand for e-commerce shipping. Mesa is also venturing into European cargo service and drone delivery to further diversify its business.

feed.title: U.S. opens probe into Amazon warehouse fatal collapse in Illinois
feed.summary: The U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) has launched an investigation into the collapse of an Amazon.com Inc warehouse in Edwardsville, Illinois, which resulted in six worker fatalities after a devastating tornado. OSHA has six months to complete its probe, issue citations, and propose penalties if workplace safety violations are found. Amazon has pledged full cooperation with the investigation, as officials also examine the building's structural integrity prior to the disaster.

feed.title: Motley Fool Global Opportunities ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:TMFG)
feed.summary: The Motley Fool Global Opportunities ETF (TMFG) invests in global companies with quality growth characteristics, selected based on qualitative and quantitative factors. The ETF, launched on June 17, 2014, is actively managed by Motley Fool and has assets under management of $354.63 million. Key holdings include Alphabet, Amazon.com, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

feed.title: Dick’s Sporting Goods launches omnichannel ‘buy now pay later’ payment
feed.summary: Dick's Sporting Goods has partnered with Afterpay to offer customers a "buy now, pay later" (BNPL) option for both in-store and online purchases. This allows shoppers to divide payments into four interest-free installments, a growing trend especially popular among younger consumers. The move makes Dick's Sporting Goods part of an expanding list of major retailers adopting BNPL services.

feed.title: Kroger taps longtime H-E-B veteran to lead Houston division
feed.summary: Kroger has appointed Laura Gump as president of its Houston division, leveraging her three decades of experience at Texas-based H-E-B and a previous role at Lowe's. Gump, who joined Kroger's Houston division in 2020, takes the helm amidst intense market competition from Walmart and H-E-B, and ongoing union negotiations with employees who recently authorized a strike. Her leadership will be critical in navigating these challenges in the Texas market, where she also serves as president of Women's Edge and on the board of the Boys and Girls Clubs of Greater Houston.

feed.title: L Catterton Asia leads Series B investment round in Nebula Brands
feed.summary: Nebula Brands, a Chinese aggregator of third-party Amazon vendors, has successfully closed a US$50 million Series B funding round led by L Catterton Asia. This investment will enable Nebula Brands to capitalize on the rapid growth of Chinese third-party Amazon vendors and expand its operations. The company aims to help vendors evolve into international brands and strengthen their operations.

feed.title: Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest Buys Over 1.5 Million Shares of Allogene
feed.summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) recently purchased over 1.5 million shares of Allogene Therapeutics Inc. (ALLO), valued at approximately $22.6 million. This acquisition occurred as the ETF experienced a more than 1% dip in Tuesday's session and a roughly 37% decline over the past year. The article details this and other transactions made by various ARK Invest ETFs on Tuesday.

feed.title: Before the Billions: The First Jobs of Some of the Richest Men in the World
feed.summary: This article explores the humble beginnings of some of the world's wealthiest individuals, highlighting their first jobs before achieving billionaire status. It details the early career paths of 15 billionaires, from Travis Kalanick selling knives door-to-door to Jeff Bezos working as a McDonald's fry cook, demonstrating that even the richest people started with ordinary or unconventional jobs. The piece also includes advice on how readers can work towards boosting their own net worth.

feed.title: T-Mobile announces $20 an hour minimum wage for all employees
feed.summary: T-Mobile has announced a new minimum wage of $20 an hour for all full-time and part-time employees, effective nationwide. CEO Mike Sievert stated this move is about "inclusion" and ensuring all employees earn a competitive wage. This decision follows similar wage hikes by other major retailers to attract and retain workers.

feed.title: Ryder grows warehouse presence near ports with $480M Whiplash deal
feed.summary: Ryder is acquiring e-commerce and omnichannel fulfillment provider Whiplash for $480 million, a move that will significantly expand Ryder's warehouse footprint near key port and gateway markets. The acquisition will add 19 distribution centers totaling nearly 7 million square feet and is expected to improve Ryder's transit times, enabling deliveries to 100% of the U.S. within two days and 60% within one day. This deal follows Ryder's recent acquisition of Midwest Warehouse & Distribution System, further solidifying its presence in strategic logistics hubs.

feed.title: Western Auto Museum is a step back in time
feed.summary: The newly established Western Auto Museum in Georgetown, Delaware, showcases the lifelong collection of Maurice Sanger, a former Western Auto store owner. The museum, located at the Nutter Marvel Carriage Museum, offers a nostalgic look at the retail chain which provided auto supplies, hardware, and various household goods before the rise of modern superstores. It features thousands of items, including rare Western Flyer merchandise, vintage auto parts, toys, and historical documents.

feed.title: Large Bellevue property sells for $78M to major biotech developer
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities has purchased a significant property in Bellevue for $77.5 million from Pine Forest Properties Inc. This acquisition marks a continued trend of investment in Bellevue, coinciding with Amazon's expansion in the area. Pine Forest Properties Inc. had previously planned a master-planned project for the site, including residential and office buildings.

feed.title: Chipotle Unveils Its First Digital Kitchen Restaurant to Fulfill Orders for Pick Up and Delivery
feed.summary: Chipotle is launching its first Chipotlane Digital Kitchen restaurant in Cuyahoga Falls, Ohio, designed specifically for digital orders with no dining room. This new smaller prototype features a Chipotlane drive-thru and a walk-up window for efficient pickup, reflecting Chipotle's strategy to expand its successful Chipotlane format and enhance its growing digital business. The company continues to invest in technology, including autonomous delivery vehicles, to increase access and convenience for guests.

feed.title: Ford CFO John Lawler Draws on His Operations Experience
feed.summary: John Lawler, who has been with Ford for over three decades, took on the role of CFO last year. His extensive tenure at the company is seen as beneficial for navigating Ford's transformation, especially concerning the shift to electric vehicles and developing new revenue streams. This article examines how Lawler's operational experience is helping Ford address upcoming challenges in the automotive industry.

feed.title: Kohl’s Announces Extended Store Hours and 40% off Last-Minute Holiday Gifts
feed.summary: Kohl's is offering extended store hours and a 40% off promotion on last-minute holiday gifts from December 17-19, with additional deals available through Christmas Eve. The retail giant is also providing convenient services like free Drive Up and In Store Pick Up, along with Kohl's Cash incentives and free shipping options to help customers complete their holiday shopping. Stores will be open until midnight for a majority of the days leading up to Christmas, closing at 6:00 p.m. on Christmas Eve.

feed.title: Silicon Labs Launches 3D Experience: The Smartest Home On The Block
feed.summary: Silicon Labs has introduced a new 3D virtual smart home platform designed to showcase interactive smart home solutions, various wireless protocols, and ecosystem connections. The experience allows users to explore different use cases like home security, home automation, and health within virtual rooms such as the front door, kitchen, bathroom, and office. This platform aims to demonstrate how Silicon Labs' technology enables more convenient, secure, and energy-efficient smart homes and will evolve with new integrations and partnerships.

feed.title: Sonos One review: still one of the best smart speakers, and an ideal multi-room starter
feed.summary: The Sonos One remains a top-tier smart speaker, offering excellent sound quality and versatile smart assistant integration with both Alexa and Google Assistant years after its debut. Despite minor drawbacks like less powerful microphones and no Bluetooth, its multi-room capabilities and wide streaming support make it an ideal choice for a platform-neutral audio experience. It stands out in its price range as a stellar starting point for a multi-room speaker system.

feed.title: Nike, Etsy’s mobile apps were ‘climbers’ this holiday season: report
feed.summary: A new report by Digital Turbine indicates that while Amazon, Walmart, and Target continue to dominate app downloads, Nike, Etsy, Poshmark, and Fetch Rewards were identified as "climbers" with above-average install intent this holiday season. This trend reflects consumers' increasing reliance on mobile apps for shopping, a behavior further accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic. Retailers are responding by enhancing mobile app features to streamline the shopping experience.

feed.title: Governor Kemp Welcomes Rivian for Single-Largest Economic Development Project in State History
feed.summary: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Rivian Inc. will invest $5 billion to build an electric adventure vehicle manufacturing plant in Georgia, creating approximately 7,500 jobs. This project, located at the East Atlanta Megasite, represents the single-largest economic development project in the state's history and aims to produce up to 400,000 vehicles annually. The facility, set to begin production in 2024, will also support Rivian's research and development programs and contribute to the expansion of the U.S. electric vehicle industry.

feed.title: Oracle in talks to buy Cerner - WSJ
feed.summary: Oracle is reportedly in talks to acquire electronic medical records company Cerner for an estimated $30 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal. This potential acquisition would be Oracle's largest ever, significantly boosting its presence in the healthcare sector and providing valuable health data for its AI-based cloud services. Both companies have yet to comment on the reported deal.

feed.title: ASX BNPL shares tumble today amid US probe
feed.summary: ASX Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) shares, including Afterpay and Zip Co, experienced a significant drop following the launch of a US investigation into the sector by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB issued orders to five BNPL companies, including Afterpay, Zip, Affirm, PayPal, and Klarna, to assess the need for better regulation and consumer protection within the rapidly growing industry. Concerns highlighted by the CFPB include the accumulation of debt, regulatory arbitrage, and data harvesting practices.

feed.title: Should you invest in Google?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Alphabet (Google) as a potential investment, highlighting its market dominance in search, strong financial performance, and diversified segments including Google Services, Google Cloud, and "Other Bets" (moonshots). It discusses the company's profitability, challenges like competition and the rise of the Metaverse, and valuation metrics, concluding that while Alphabet has strong fundamentals, investors should monitor its growth trajectory and margins. The article also explains the different share classes (GOOGL and GOOG).

feed.title: Verizon, T-Mobile Shun Public Clouds for Network Infrastructure
feed.summary: Verizon and T-Mobile are opting against using public clouds for their core network functions, choosing instead to rely on private, in-house cloud platforms. This strategy is driven by the need for extreme reliability and security for critical services, a stance reinforced by recent Amazon Web Services outages. In contrast, AT&T is moving its 5G core and network workloads entirely to Microsoft Azure.

feed.title: Find Out Where Every ‘Spider-Man’ Movie is Streaming – Either for Free or Rental
feed.summary: This article lists where to stream every 'Spider-Man' movie, including those starring Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland. It details which subscription services offer the films for free and where they can be rented, noting that "Spider-Man: No Way Home" was only in theaters at the time of publication. The piece also explains why Spider-Man movies are not all on Disney+ with the rest of the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

feed.title: United Natural Foods’ Legal Chief Exits After Job Scope Narrowed
feed.summary: United Natural Foods Inc. (UNFI) announced the departure of its top lawyer, Jill Sutton, after narrowing her job responsibilities. Sutton's duties were redistributed to other departments to streamline the organizational structure, leading to a mutual agreement for her exit. UNFI acknowledged Sutton's contributions since joining in 2018.

feed.title: 1 Dead in Fire at Sprawling QVC Distribution Center (Published 2021)
feed.summary: A massive fire at a QVC distribution center in Rocky Mount, N.C., resulted in one death and damaged up to 75% of the 1.2 million-square-foot facility. Approximately 300 employees were present when the fire began, and the victim, initially unaccounted for, was later found deceased inside the building. The incident is under investigation, and its impact on the local economy, where QVC is a major employer, is a significant concern.

feed.title: You could be a landlord for Amazon, FedEx and Walmart with these REITs that net up to a 4.4% yield — you can even collect on a monthly basis
feed.summary: The article discusses the investment opportunity in Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) that act as landlords for major companies like Amazon, FedEx, and Walmart, offering attractive dividend yields. It highlights STAG Industrial, which rents to Amazon and pays monthly dividends, and Realty Income, which has a diverse portfolio including Walmart and FedEx and boasts a long history of monthly dividend increases. The author also suggests looking into alternative assets for diversification beyond the stock market.

feed.title: A unicorn in South Burlington: Kyle Clark and BETA Technologies
feed.summary: This article profiles Kyle Clark, CEO of BETA Technologies, an electric flight pioneer based in South Burlington, Vermont. Clark, a Harvard graduate and former professional hockey player, has developed electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, initially focusing on cargo and medical deliveries. The company has raised $400 million, is valued at $1.4 billion, and is building a 355,000-square-foot assembly site, with customers including the US Department of Defense, UPS, and United Therapeutics.

feed.title: Rivian to Invest in Single-Largest Economic Development Project in Georgia History
feed.summary: Rivian announced a $5 billion investment in Georgia to construct a carbon-conscious campus for manufacturing electric adventure vehicles, creating approximately 7,500 jobs. This will be the single-largest economic development project in the state's history, with production slated to begin in 2024 and an annual capacity of up to 400,000 vehicles. The project highlights Georgia's focus on electric mobility and sustainable development, leveraging its logistics, workforce, and infrastructure.

feed.title: Apple Should Make a Giant iPad as Its Smart Home Portal
feed.summary: After testing Amazon's new Echo Show 15, the author suggests Apple Inc. (AAPL) should develop a larger iPad to serve as a smart home hub. This move would enhance Apple's presence in the smart home market and diversify the utility of its iPad line. The article also touches on other Apple-related news, including COVID-19 disruptions and internal company changes.

feed.title: At Waters, a digital workplace strategy pays off amid crisis
feed.summary: Waters Corp. CIO Brook Colangelo's decision to migrate the company to cloud-based productivity and collaboration software before the coronavirus pandemic proved prescient, enabling its 7,000 employees to stay connected and secure while working from home. This digital workplace strategy, supported by a modified Spotify "squad" model, improved the employee experience and allowed the company to handle an increased volume of online engagements and Microsoft Teams usage during the crisis. Colangelo emphasizes thinking like a platform leader, adopting a growth mindset, and obsessing over user adoption for a successful digital workplace.

feed.title: Amazon silenced criticism of Xi to do business in China: Report
feed.summary: A Reuters report alleges that Amazon.com Inc. quieted criticism of President Xi Jinping's book on its Chinese platform to maintain business in China. This involved scrubbing negative ratings and reviews for "The Governance of China" following requests from Beijing. This situation mirrors cases of other US companies like Apple and contrasts with others like Yahoo! and LinkedIn that have exited the Chinese market due to a challenging business environment.

feed.title: 5 things to know about Oracle after $28.3 billion acquisition of Kansas City’s Cerner
feed.summary: Oracle has announced a $28.3 billion acquisition of Cerner Corp., Kansas City's largest private employer, marking one of the year's biggest deals. This move signifies Oracle's growing push into the healthcare industry, following similar expansions by other tech giants. The acquisition brings together Oracle, an $280 billion company with 133,000 employees globally, and Cerner, valued at $23 billion with 28,000 employees worldwide, notably 13,000 in Kansas City.

feed.title: Oracle to acquire Cerner in $28.3B deal
feed.summary: Oracle announced its acquisition of medical record technology provider Cerner in an all-cash deal valued at $28.3 billion, or $95 per share. This strategic move aims to expand Oracle's presence in the healthcare sector and boost its cloud business globally, with the transaction expected to close in 2022. Oracle CEO Safra Catz anticipates the deal will be a significant revenue growth engine, while chairman Larry Ellison highlighted the potential to reduce administrative burdens for medical professionals and improve patient care.

feed.title: Radnor-based renewable energy pioneer is sold to Fortune 500 company
feed.summary: Community Energy Inc., a Radnor-based renewable energy pioneer, has sold its solar energy development business, Community Energy Solar LLC, to AES Corp., an international energy company. This acquisition will increase AES's renewable project pipeline by about a third to 40 GW and allow Community Energy to accelerate its growth in response to increasing demand for solar energy. Community Energy's leadership will maintain a presence in Radnor while also exploring new climate technology ventures.

feed.title: Sony Pictures Consumer Products Expands Global Merchandise Program Ahead of Cobra Kai Season 4 Debut for Fans to Create Show-Themed New Year’s Eve Celebrations
feed.summary: Sony Pictures Consumer Products (SPCP) is significantly expanding its global merchandise program for "Cobra Kai" in anticipation of the show's fourth season debut on December 31st. The extensive product line, covering apparel, toys, collectibles, home goods, and more, is available internationally and includes new collaborations with HERO Beverage Co. and Champion Athleticwear. Fans can find show-themed items to support their favorite dojos, just in time for New Year's Eve watch parties.

feed.title: Oracle to buy Cerner for $28.3 bln in healthcare sector push
feed.summary: Oracle announced its acquisition of Cerner for $28.3 billion, marking its largest deal ever and a significant push into the healthcare sector. This acquisition gives Oracle access to a vast amount of healthcare data, which it plans to leverage for its AI-based cloud services and to migrate Cerner's software to its own cloud platform. The deal is expected to close in 2022 and contribute to Oracle's revenue growth and earnings.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc: Report on Aligning Retirement Plan Options with Company Climate Goals
feed.summary: A shareholder resolution requests Amazon's Board to review its retirement plan options to ensure alignment with the company's climate action goals. The resolution highlights that Amazon's 401(k) plan, including its default option, invests heavily in fossil fuel and deforestation-risk companies, contradicting Amazon's net-zero carbon pledges. It suggests providing more sustainable investment options for employees to mitigate reputational risk and cognitive dissonance.

feed.title: Amazon plans major expansion in Austin, to hire 2,000 workers
feed.summary: Amazon announced a significant expansion in Austin, planning to create over 2,000 corporate and tech jobs in the coming years. To support this growth, the e-commerce giant has leased 330,000 square feet of office space at the Domain, with the new facility expected to open in early 2024. This expansion is part of Amazon's broader national growth strategy and reflects Austin's continued attractiveness to major tech companies.

feed.title: Oracle Cerner Acquisition Opens the Door for Deeper Inroads into the Healthcare Data Business
feed.summary: Oracle has announced its acquisition of Cerner Corp for $28.3 billion, marking its largest deal to date and signifying a major push into the healthcare data business. This strategic move aims to solidify Oracle's presence in the cloud and healthcare sectors, leveraging Cerner's electronic medical records expertise and addressing the rising demand for cloud-based healthcare solutions. The acquisition is viewed as a power move that allows Oracle to compete more effectively with other tech giants in the cloud arena while enabling Cerner to continue its transformation and growth.

feed.title: BD Announces New Distribution and Retail Partners for BD Veritor™ At-Home COVID-19 Test
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) announced new distribution and retail partners for its BD Veritor™ At-Home COVID-19 Test, making it available through Everly Health and Southeastern Grocers (Winn-Dixie, Harveys Supermarket, and Fresco y Más). The test utilizes a smartphone camera and app for result interpretation and is among the few at-home tests to automate reporting to public health agencies. This expansion aims to enhance access to reliable at-home COVID-19 testing.

feed.title: Sony, Zee finalise merger even as courtroom feud continues
feed.summary: Sony's India unit and local rival Zee Entertainment have finalized a merger deal to combine their television channels, film assets, and streaming platforms. This alliance aims to compete with major streaming players like Netflix and Disney in India and will create the country's largest media entity, with Sony holding the majority stake. The merger proceeds despite an ongoing courtroom battle between Zee's founders and its largest shareholder, Invesco, over the company's management and leadership.

feed.title: P&G shampoo recall is only the latest for benzene in spray products
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble has issued a recall for aerosol dry shampoo and conditioner spray products due to the presence of benzene, a known carcinogen. This recall affects several brands including Aussie, Herbal Essences, Pantene, and Waterless, and is part of a larger trend of benzene contamination in various spray products, such as sunscreens and hand sanitizers. P&G states that exposure to the detected levels of benzene in their products is not expected to cause adverse health consequences, and they are offering reimbursement for recalled items.

feed.title: Amazon Cloud Unit Draws Antitrust Scrutiny From Khan’s FTC
feed.summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission, led by Lina Khan, is actively pursuing an antitrust investigation into Amazon.com Inc.'s cloud computing business, Amazon Web Services (AWS). This probe, which originated under Khan's predecessor, shows increased scrutiny of the online retailer's various operations. Federal investigators have reportedly contacted several companies as part of this ongoing inquiry.

feed.title: Amazon warehouse workers in New York City refile union petition
feed.summary: Amazon warehouse workers in New York City have refiled a petition to unionize four facilities on Staten Island after gathering enough signatures. This move follows a previous failed attempt and comes amid a broader U.S. labor movement, including successful unionization efforts at Starbucks. The group is demanding the reinstatement of hazard pay and the permanent ability for employees to keep their phones on the job.

feed.title: Gildan Activewear Acquires Yarn Supplier for $168 Million
feed.summary: Gildan Activewear has acquired Frontier Yarns for $168 million in an all-cash deal, expanding its East Coast U.S. manufacturing operation and further internalizing yarn production. This acquisition strengthens Gildan's vertically integrated supply chain and supports its textile capacity expansion plans in Central America and the Caribbean, leveraging Frontier Yarns' existing role as a key supplier. The deal comes as Gildan generated significant free cash flow and strong sales, demonstrating the success of its "Back to Basics" plan.

feed.title: Capital One to pay $190M settlement in data breach linked to Seattle woman
authors: The Seattle Times Staff & News services
feed.summary: Capital One has agreed to a $190 million settlement for a class-action lawsuit filed by customers affected by a 2019 data breach. The breach involved data from approximately 100 million people and was reportedly carried out by a former Amazon Web Services employee from Seattle. The settlement aims to resolve all claims from the lawsuit, though Capital One and AWS deny liability.

feed.title: Amazon workers in the US now face fewer hurdles to organise
feed.summary: Amazon has reached a settlement with the U.S. National Labor Relations Board, requiring the company to inform past and current workers of their rights and to not interfere with labor organizing on company property after hours. This agreement is expected to significantly ease the process for Amazon employees to organize against their employer, addressing practices previously used by Amazon to hinder such efforts. The settlement coincides with increased labor activity at Amazon facilities nationwide, including a union-organizing effort in New York City.

feed.title: Takeda and AWS accelerate COVID-19 research push
feed.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical is partnering with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Accenture to accelerate its digital transformation, moving 80% of its applications to the public cloud over the next five years. This collaboration aims to boost Takeda's R&D pipeline for drug discovery, including therapies for COVID-19, and enable faster data sharing among pharmaceutical companies for clinical trials. The initiative is also addressing internal needs by improving plasma collection capacity and developing employee return-to-work applications.

feed.title: Capital One (COF) Settles Class-Action Cyber Lawsuit for $190 Million
feed.summary: Capital One Financial Corp. has agreed to pay $190 million to settle a class-action lawsuit following a cyberattack that exposed customer personal information. The settlement is intended to cover 98 million Americans whose data was compromised. Capital One stated it has fully reserved for the settlement amount.

feed.title: 32% of Growth to Originate from APAC for Data Analytics Market|Evolving Opportunities with Amazon Web Services Inc., International Business Machines Corp. & Microsoft Corp. |17000+ Technavio Reports
feed.summary: The data analytics market is projected to grow by USD 196.47 billion from 2021 to 2026, with a CAGR of 13.54%. A significant 32% of this growth is expected to come from the APAC region, particularly from China and India. The market's expansion is driven by the extensive use of modern technology in company operations, though challenges like integrating data from various sources may temper growth.

feed.title: Don’t Buy BABA. It’s Not What You Think
feed.summary: This article warns investors against buying Chinese stocks, particularly Alibaba (BABA), due to inherent structural risks. The author explains that US investors buying shares of Chinese companies like BABA are often acquiring shares of Cayman Islands-based holding companies, not direct equity in the Chinese operating entity, which offers minimal shareholder protection. The piece cites examples like DiDi Global Inc. (DIDI) and RLX Technology Inc. (RLX) to illustrate the significant risks involved with these investments.
